news.Title: Why Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller Dumped Nvidia but Loaded Up on These 3 AI Infrastructure Stocks for the Next Boom
news.Content:
Stanley Druckenmiller's moves have been particularly telling throughout the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution. After initiating a position in Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) during the early generative AI surge, the billionaire money manager fully exited the position by late 2024.

During the first quarter of 2026, his Duquesne Family Office initiated new stakes in Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) , Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) , and Arm Holdings (NASDAQ: ARM) . This is no random reshuffling. These decisions reflect a deliberate bet that a new layer within the AI chip stack is emerging -- moving away from Nvidia's general-purpose training GPUs (graphic processing units) and shifting toward greater adoption of custom silicon and central processing units (CPUs).

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

Why did Stanley Druckenmiller sell Nvidia stock?

Druckenmiller bought 582,915 shares of Nvidia during the fourth quarter of 2022. During the quarters that followed, he traded around Nvidia as the rise of ChatGPT sparked an AI frenzy featuring the chipmaker's indispensable GPUs.

Between ChatGPT's commercial launch (Nov. 30, 2022) and the end of the third quarter of 2024, Nvidia stock had risen more than 600%, eventually becoming the world's most valuable company.

Even to a bull like Druckenmiller, this appeared as a clear example of extreme valuation expansion. Liquidating his position was a prudent demonstration of portfolio management: Take profits when prices get ahead of fundamentals, even in a compelling story stock such as Nvidia.

Druckenmiller said selling Nvidia was a mistake

During an interview with Bloomberg in October 2024, Druckenmiller admitted that he sold Nvidia too early -- saying the move was a "big mistake." He praised Nvidia and alluded that his fund would consider buying the stock again if the valuation became reasonable.

Nevertheless, by mid-2026, Nvidia's valuation profile had moderated from its peaks, yet Druckenmiller still remained on the sidelines. Instead of returning to the GPU leader, he appears to be shifting capital into alternative silicon architectures positioned for what could be the next leg of the AI infrastructure boom.

The inference era is here

According to Duquesne's Q1 13F, the fund bought 195,955 shares of Broadcom, 411,400 shares of Intel, and 106,700 shares of Arm. I see this as a signal of Druckenmiller's conviction that inference, not training, will drive the bulk of AI compute spending in the years ahead.

Story Continues

Training large language models (LLMs) is a one-time task suited for Nvidia's GPUs. However, inference means running these models for users in real time to make predictions and estimates, thereby demanding something different: greater power efficiency, lower cost per query, and customization for specific workloads.

Hyperscalers and AI labs are responding by designing their own custom silicon chips and leaning into optimized CPU architectures. These chips consume less energy and cost less than general-purpose GPUs when performing inference at scale.

The shift away from GPUs is already becoming visible in multibillion-dollar commitments from Alphabet , Meta Platforms , Amazon , Microsoft , OpenAI, and Anthropic . Broadcom is the leader in this wave of custom application specific integrated circuits (ASICs). The company partners with some of the biggest cloud infrastructure providers, such as Google Cloud, to co-design and manufacture its specialized accelerators -- known as tensor processing units (TPUs).

Intel is undergoing a renaissance as inference workloads increasingly run on traditional processors and hybrid systems. The company's Xeon 6 and x86 CPUs are quietly becoming the backbone of enterprise data centers, including those managed by Alphabet and Nvidia.

Meanwhile, Arm supplies efficient core architectures that power custom chip designs and data center CPUs. The company's licensing model allows chipmakers and cloud platforms to build low-power, high-bandwidth solutions ideal for inference.

Taken together, these three holdings give Druckenmiller vast exposure to the pick-and-shovel players who will profit as AI development transitions from the training phase to the realities surrounding high-volume model deployment.

Was Druckenmiller's move savvy? I think so. These moves clearly indicate that Druckenmiller is not abandoning the AI trade. He is simply repositioning beyond the obvious macro winners. Within the infrastructure layer, he appears to be betting that custom silicon and CPUs will prove to be durable alternatives to GPUs as big tech continues to pour vast amounts of capital into inference workloads.

news.Title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Just Became Too Cheap to Ignore
news.Content:
Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) hasn't been an exciting growth stock to own lately. It's trailing all of its fellow "Magnificent Seven" stocks so far in 2026, with a 13% year-to-date decline. It's also down by more than 20% from its all-time high.

However, that tumble is a buying opportunity. Microsoft's fundamentals have continued to improve, and it trades at a P/E ratio of just 25, which is a lower valuation than every Magnificent Seven stock except Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

Investors have come to expect solid earnings

Microsoft doesn't deliver the type of growth you'll find in Nvidia 's press releases, but it still produces solid results for long-term investors. The tech giant reported an 18% year-over-year revenue jump in its fiscal 2026 third quarter, which ended March 31. Net income increased by 23% year over year.

Microsoft Cloud was the major catalyst fueling those numbers. Businesses rely on Microsoft's cloud platform for scalable IT infrastructure and pay monthly fees to continue using the platform.

Once a company has set its operations up using Microsoft Cloud, it is difficult to switch to another cloud provider. Companies that do so may have to rebuild parts of their digital infrastructure that relied on Azure-specific tools and retrain employees on how to use the replacement cloud platform. Businesses prefer to avoid those types of headaches, so that recurring revenue is fairly reliable. Between established clients and new ones, Microsoft has been able to deliver steady cloud revenue growth.

Microsoft also has other business segments that continue to gain ground. For example, LinkedIn revenue climbed by 12% year over year, while search advertising revenue achieved the same growth rate.

Agentic AI presents a new opportunity

Investors regularly look at Microsoft Cloud's numbers to assess how the company will perform in future quarters. However, they may also want to focus on Microsoft's AI business, which surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion in fiscal Q3. That figure was up 123% year over year. That averages out to a little more than $9 billion per quarter, which would have represented more than 10% of Microsoft's fiscal Q3 revenue.

CEO Satya Nadella cited agentic computing when describing the AI business and how it helps other companies. Enterprise agentic AI is a lucrative industry, and Grand View Research projects that the space will grow at a compound annual rate of 46.2% from now through 2030.

Story Continues

That tailwind may be just what gives Microsoft the momentum to get back to delivering revenue growth of 20% or more in future quarters. Such a result would see Microsoft following in the footsteps of Amazon and Alphabet , both of which have achieved meaningful revenue growth accelerations in recent quarters.

Microsoft is already well positioned to ride the agentic AI industry's growth. Its Agent 365 software helps companies create secure AI agents. Microsoft mentioned this product in a recent blog post that outlined how companies and institutions like Air India, cybersecurity company ContraForce, and the school district of Broward County in Florida are using Microsoft's AI agents to save millions of dollars while improving their operations.

Those are meaningful wins that will keep businesses in the Microsoft ecosystem. And such successes also give Microsoft greater flexibility to charge higher prices and attract more customers as positive case studies become more common.

news.Title: Prediction: Micron Technology Stock Will Be Worth at Least $1,500 in 1 Year.
news.Content:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) has witnessed a jaw-dropping surge in its stock price over the past month, rising from $448 to a peak of $804. That run-up reflected growing excitement around artificial intelligence (AI) and the critical roles that DRAM and NAND memory chips play in next-generation data centers.

As hyperscalers accelerate their investments into AI infrastructure, demand for advanced memory solutions has risen sharply, to levels well in excess of supply. Micron's ability to deliver strong revenue and earnings results amid this memory supercycle has shifted sentiment -- turning what was once viewed as a cyclical commodity player into a company that is now viewed as a key enabler of the AI boom.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

What fueled Micron's recent surge?

The parabolic move in Micron's stock stemmed from exploding demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and other advanced DRAM and NAND products. GPUs and other AI processors need rapid access to large volumes of data when they are training and deploying large language models (LLMs), and Micron's offerings can supply that.

As AI data center operators face intensifying pressure to scale up their computing capacity, one of the bottlenecks controlling the pace of their build-outs is the available supply of specialized memory. This environment has allowed Micron to command strong pricing power, expand its footprint in AI data centers, and grow both revenue and profits impressively.

What does Micron's growth trajectory look like?

The traditional boom-and-bust pattern in the memory market arises from the nature of the business. Memory-chip makers engage in heavy capital investments to add production capacity during periods of higher demand, but it takes a few years to get those new factories up and running. The combination of that lag and the moves of all the competitors in the space to boost their capacity has always led to periods of oversupply that result in slumping prices -- collapses that can be exacerbated if consumers and enterprises broadly choose not to upgrade their devices, further sapping demand.

However, the AI revolution has introduced meaningful secular tailwinds that should alleviate some of the cyclical volatility. Expanding AI workloads, the rise of autonomous vehicles and robotics, and the introduction of agentic AI should provide a more robust floor for memory demand compared to past cycles.

Story Continues

Micron's focus on delivering higher-value products like HBM has translated into improving visibility from customer commitments, offering greater earnings stability. The company is investing heavily in next-generation HBM4E while also maintaining prudent financial discipline. In my view, Micron's rally appears sustainable in the medium term due to the multiyear runway of the AI infrastructure build-out.

The path to $1,500

Analysts project strong earnings growth for Micron over the next couple of years. For its fiscal 2027, which will begin in late August, earnings projections climb significantly toward $103 as production scales and pricing dynamics remain favorable. At current trading levels, Micron's forward price-to-earnings (P/E) multiple of about 12 appears attractive.

Simple math shows that applying a 15x multiple to a $103 fiscal 2027 EPS target results in a share price of about $1,500. Such a move would represent roughly 105% upside from Micron's current price of around $730.

While it's ambitious, I think my scenario aligns with the company's earnings trajectory should AI adoption continue at its current rapid pace and Micron maintain a competitive edge. In fact, I think there is a solid chance that Micron will outperform Wall Street's estimates.

There's growing conviction among investors that the AI memory boom is here to stay. So while predicting this level of stock price appreciation may appear aggressive, I think it is justifiable for a company that boasts sustained demand from hyperscalers and has a proven ability to build out its production capacity.

news.Title: COMPUTEXе∞ЗзЩїе†і ж≥ХдЇЇзЬЛе•љеП∞иВ°еЛХиГљеПѓжЬЫеїґиЗ≥дЄЛеНКеєі
news.Content:
еП∞еМЧеЬЛйЪЫйЫїиЕ¶е±Х(COMPUTEX)е∞ЗжЦЉ6жЬИ2жЧ•иЗ≥5жЧ•жЦЉеП∞еМЧеНЧжЄѓе±Хи¶љй§®зЩїе†іпЉМиЉЭйБФеЯЈи°МйХЈйїГдїБеЛ≥жПРеЙНдЊЖеП∞пЉМзВЇеЄВе†іеЕИйїЮзЗГCOMPUTEXиИЗ AI дЊЫжЗЙйПИи°МжГЕгАВж≥ХдЇЇи™НзВЇпЉМе§Ци≥ЗйХЈжЬЯзЬЛе•љAIиґ®еЛҐпЉМдїКеєіеП∞зБ£дЄКеЄВжЂГдЉБж•≠зН≤еИ©жХійЂФдЊЖзЬЛпЉМеПѓжЬЫиЉГ2025еєіжИРйХЈиґЕйБО1еАНпЉМеП∞иВ°еЛХиГљеПѓжЬЫеїґзЇМиЗ≥дЄЛеНКеєігАВ

еН≥е∞ЗзЩїе†ізЪД2026еєіCOMPUTEXпЉМдїКеєідЄїй°МеЃЪи™њзВЇгАМAI TogetherгАНпЉМиБЪзД¶AIеЯЇз§ОеїЇи®≠иИЗжЗЙзФ®иРљеЬ∞пЉМй†РжЬЯеЊЮжЩґзЙЗйБЛзЃЧиГљеКЫгАБдЉЇжЬНеЩ®жЮґжІЛеИ∞зµВзЂѓжЗЙзФ®пЉМйГље∞ЗеЃМжХіе±ХзПЊAIзФЯжЕЛз≥їзЩЉе±ХжЦєеРСгАВеЄВе†ідєЯй†РжЬЯпЉМеЬЛйЪЫе§Іеї†еЬ®е±ХжЬГжЬЯйЦУеЕђеЄГзЪДжЦ∞зФҐеУБиИЗз≠ЦзХ•еЄГе±АпЉМе∞ЗйА≤дЄАж≠•жПРеНЗеЄВе†іе∞НAIйХЈзЈЪжИРйХЈзЪДдњ°ењГгАВ

AIжЩґзЙЗйЬЄдЄїиЉЭйБФ(NVIDIA)еЯЈи°МйХЈйїГдїБеЛ≥жПРеЙНжКµеП∞пЉМеЄВе†ідєЯйїЮзЗГCOMPUTEXиИЗAIдЊЫжЗЙйПИи°МжГЕпЉМеП∞еМЧиВ°еЄВдЄКйА±дЇФжФґзЫ§жЩВзЂЩдЄК42267йїЮзЪДжФґзЫ§жЦ∞йЂШгАВ

еЬЛеЕІж≥ХдЇЇжМЗеЗЇпЉМйЫЦзДґ2026еєіеЕ®зРГзґУжњЯжИРйХЈеЛХиГљиЉГеЙНеєЊеєізХ•зВЇжФЊзЈ©пЉМдљЖеЬ®дЄїи¶БзґУжњЯйЂФдїНеЕЈйЯМжАІжФѓжТРдЄЛпЉМжХійЂФжЩѓж∞£зґ≠жМБгАМжФЊзЈ©дљЖз©©еБ•гАНж†Ље±АпЉМAIдїНжШѓдїКеєіжЬАж†ЄењГзЪДжИРйХЈдЄїиїЄгАВйЪ®иСЧеЬЛйЪЫзІСжКАе§Іеї†жМБзЇМжУіе§ІAIи≥ЗжЬђжФѓеЗЇпЉМеК†дЄКCOMPUTEXеН≥е∞ЗзЩїе†іпЉМAIдЊЫжЗЙйПИзЖ±еЇ¶жЬЙжЬЫеїґзЇМиЗ≥дЄЛеНКеєігАВйЗОжЭСжКХдњ°жКХи≥Зз≠ЦзХ•йГ®еЙѓзЄљзґУзРЖж®УеЕЛжЬЫи™™пЉЪгАО(еОЯйЯ≥)жИСеАСи™НзВЇеЕґеѓ¶еЊИе§Ъе§Ци≥ЗеЯЇжЬђдЄКйХЈжЬЯйГљйВДжШѓзЬЛе•љAIзЪДиґ®еЛҐпЉМжИСеАСи™НзВЇзХґеЬ∞зЈ£жФњж≤їзЪДйАЩз®ЃзЯ≠жЬЯзЪДељ±йЯњпЉМе¶ВжЮЬиГље§†еЊЧеИ∞зЈ©иІ£зЪДи©±пЉМеЕґеѓ¶и≥ЗйЗСжЬГеЊИењЂзЪДж≠ЄдљНпЉМжИСеАСжЬГзЬЛеИ∞жЬ™дЊЖеЕґеѓ¶е§ІеЃґйВДжШѓеПѓдї•зЬЛеИ∞еП∞иВ°дЄАеАЛжѓФиЉГжЬЭеРСеПНжШ†еЯЇжЬђйЭҐзЪДж¶ЃжЩѓеОїзЩЉе±ХзЪДдЄАеАЛжЕЛеЛҐгАВгАП

ж®УеЕЛжЬЫжМЗеЗЇпЉМAIдїНжШѓ2026еєіжЬАйЗНи¶БзЪДзФҐж•≠еЛХиГљгАВеЕ®зРГйЫ≤зЂѓжЬНеЛЩеХЖиИЗзІСжКАйЊНй†≠жМБзЇМжУіе§ІAIжКХи≥ЗпЉМеЊЮж®°еЮЛи®УзЈіеИ∞жО®иЂЦйЬАж±ВеРМж≠•еҐЮеК†пЉМеЄґеЛХAIдЉЇжЬНеЩ®гАБйЂШйАЯйБЛзЃЧиИЗи≥ЗжЦЩдЄ≠ењГеїЇи®≠йЬАж±ВжМБзЇМеНЗжЇЂгАВе∞§еЕґеП∞з©НйЫїеЕИеЙНж≥Хи™™жЬГйЗЛеЗЇж≠£еРСе±ХжЬЫеЊМпЉМдєЯйА≤дЄАж≠•зҐЇи™НAIзЫЄйЧЬйЬАж±ВдїНеЕЈеЉЈеЛБеЛХиГљпЉМй†РжЬЯзЫЄйЧЬжКХи≥Зе∞ЗеїґзЇМиЗ≥2026еєідЄЛеНКеєігАВ

йЗОжЭСжКХдњ°з≠ЦзХ•жЪ®и°МйКЈиЩХи≥ЗжЈ±еЙѓзЄљеЉµзєЉжЦЗдєЯжМЗеЗЇпЉМиЉЭйБФ22жЧ•еЕђеЄГзЪДи≤°е†±и°®зПЊйЭЮеЄЄе•љпЉМдєЯйА≤дЄАж≠•зҐЇи™НжЬ™дЊЖAIзЩЉе±Хиґ®еЛҐпЉМзЙєеИ•жШѓиЉЭйБФжЬАжЦ∞дЄАдї£е∞ИзВЇдї£зРЖAIжЙУйА†зЪДиґЕзіЪйЫїиЕ¶еє≥еП∞Vera RubinдЄКеЄВйКЈеФЃпЉМжЫіиГљзВЇеП∞зБ£дЊЫжЗЙйПИеЄґдЊЖжЫіе§ІзЪДеЉЈеЛБжИРйХЈеЛХиГљгАВ

news.Title: Where Will Costco Be in 5 Years?
news.Content:
There are a handful of giants in the retail world, with Walmart and Amazon as the household names leading the way. Walmart stores are accessible to the vast majority of the American population, and Amazon is the undisputed e-commerce king, but neither runs the warehouse memberships like Costco (NASDAQ: COST) .

Costco's stock hasn't gotten as much attention as tech or artificial intelligence (AI) stocks over the past few years. Still, it has outperformed all major indexes and every " Magnificent Seven " stock not named Nvidia or Alphabet over the past five years.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

But while past success is nice, future success matters. Where will Costco and its stock be in five years ?

Embracing different property styles to remove expansion barriers

Costco's memberships are growing -- which is a good thing, no doubt -- but one downside is overcrowded stores in high-volume areas. To help with the issue (and overall growth), Costco is pursuing an aggressive expansion plan, aiming to open at least 30 new stores annually.

For your average city, it's a straightforward process once you find space for it (Costco aims for at least 25 acres). In high-density cities like New York City and Los Angeles, space isn't readily available to build large Costco stores, so the company is embracing mixed-use properties that deviate from its traditional designs but allow it to capitalize on high-income areas.

Costco is currently building its first mixed-use property in Los Angeles, with apartment buildings atop the warehouse, and the company says such properties should increase foot traffic. If these mixed-use stores in high-density areas prove effective, the burden of space will become less meaningful for Costco's physical footprint. It currently has 28 stores in its pipeline for this year.

Making China a major part of its international expansion

Costco currently has 924 warehouses, most of which (634) are in the U.S. The company has seven stores in mainland China that essentially serve as initial trial runs for the company. Now, it's planning to make China a larger part of its international growth plans.

Costco's U.S. growth is modest, but its international growth is much faster. In its fiscal second quarter (ended Feb. 15), comparable U.S. sales increased 5.9%, while international sales increased 13%. China won't be a magic market that skyrockets Costco's growth, but if it plays out how Costco plans, it will be a significant part of the company's international growth going forward.

Story Continues

So, where will the stock be in five years?

I can't predict how any stock will perform, but if Costco's business continues to thrive at its current level, it's safe to assume the stock will reward it accordingly.

The one issue with the stock is that it's currently expensive (a nearly 56 price-to-earnings ratio recently), which means it's at higher risk of a pullback or slowdown if investors become impatient with the company's progress. However, for long-term investors, the stock is a buy.

Costco has found its place in the retail world and is building brand equity daily.

news.Title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang Just Delivered Big News to Shareholders. And It May Influence Your Decision on Whether to Buy Nvidia Stock Right Now.
news.Content:
Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has seen earnings explode higher quarter after quarter as tech giants rush to get in on its latest artificial intelligence (AI) systems. And that has led to outstanding stock performance -- the shares have climbed more than 600% over the past three years as this AI revolution heats up.

But in recent months, investors have expressed concern about one thing in particular. They've worried about whether the high levels of demand will last. Major cloud providers such as Microsoft, Amazon , and other tech leaders have pledged to spend nearly $700 billion this year on infrastructure build-out, and this, of course, is benefiting chip designers such as Nvidia. The concern, though, is that any slowdown in the pace of such spending could do just the opposite -- and weigh on growth.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

Considering all of this, investors have been particularly tuned in to any messages from tech giants that may offer insight into what's to come. Nvidia chief Jensen Huang, in this week's earnings call, just delivered big news to shareholders -- and it may influence your decision on whether to buy Nvidia stock right now.

Nvidia in the AI environment

First, though, let's take a quick look at the AI story so far and how Nvidia has evolved in this environment. In the earliest stages of the AI boom, customers focused on training large language models -- and to pour incredible amounts of information into these models at high speeds, they needed compute. The ideal compute came in the form of Nvidia's graphics processing unit (GPU).

While other AI chips may also fuel training, Nvidia's GPUs have done it faster and more efficiently than any other. Customers rushed to get in on these powerful tools and complete AI systems, and this has supercharged Nvidia's revenue growth.

As mentioned above, after such high levels of growth and demand, investors have questioned whether Nvidia's best days may be behind the company. The idea is that, though major tech customers are spending big on infrastructure now, this may not last forever. It's important to note, however, that AI doesn't end with training -- in fact, that's just the beginning.

And this leads me to the big news Huang delivered to shareholders this past week.

"Demand has gone parabolic," Huang said. "The reason is simple. Agentic AI has arrived. AI can now do productive and valuable work."

Story Continues

Need for GPUs and CPUs

AI agents use the knowledge from all of that training to take action and complete tasks. And the key point here is to do that work, AI continues to need compute in the form of GPUs -- and central processing units (CPUs). Nvidia has this covered with its current Blackwell system and its newest platform, Vera Rubin. Tailored for the needs of AI agents, Rubin is set to ship in the third quarter of this year.

On top of this, customers continue to flock to the current Blackwell platform, so Blackwell and Rubin are positioned to propel Nvidia's growth in the quarters to come. The company says the number of partner data centers that have surpassed 10 megawatts has almost doubled in 12 months.

What does this mean for investors? Huang clearly delivered positive news, and this is news that should also relieve the worries of investors who questioned the future growth opportunity. We're seeing that the need for compute is ongoing and may even increase as more and more companies actually apply AI to real-world situations.

Meanwhile, the recent worries about the sustainability of demand weighed on Nvidia stock in the first quarter and, as a result, lowered its valuation. Even though the stock has since rebounded, valuation remains very interesting. The stock trades for 25x forward earnings estimates, down from 40x at the start of the year.

So, after this big news from Jensen Huang, Nvidia stock looks like a buy.

news.Title: 3 Monster Dividend Stocks to Hold for the Next 10 Years
news.Content:
"If you aren't willing to own a stock for 10 years, don't even think about owning it for 10 minutes."

That's the advice from legendary investor Warren Buffett. His point was that you should have enough confidence in the underlying business that you're comfortable holding the stock for the long term.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

The good news is that there are plenty of stocks that warrant such confidence -- and many of them pay attractive dividends. Here are three monster dividend stocks to hold for the next 10 years.

1. AbbVie

AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV) is a leading pharmaceutical company. Its product lineup features multiple blockbuster drugs, such as Skyrizi, Rinvoq, Humira, Botox, Vraylar, Imbruvica, and Venclexta. AbbVie's pipeline includes around 90 programs, of which roughly 60 are in mid- or late-stage clinical trials.

The drugmaker is a member of the Dividend Kings , an elite group of stocks that have increased their dividends for at least 50 consecutive years. AbbVie's streak of dividend hikes stands at 53 years, including the time it was part of Abbott Labs (NYSE: ABT) . The company's forward dividend yield tops 3.2%.

I view this stock as one to hold for the next 10 years largely because its growth prospects are compelling. The autoimmune disease drugs Skyrizi and Rinvoq have particularly strong growth potential. Several of AbbVie's cancer therapies in development are also promising.

AbbVie has also demonstrated its ability to adapt effectively to daunting challenges. The company's longtime top-selling product, Humira, lost U.S. patent exclusivity in 2023. But AbbVie's growth barely skipped a beat, thanks to its investments in research and development and smart acquisitions that added new growth drivers. The drugmaker now appears to be in a great position to cruise over the next decade and beyond.

2. Enterprise Products Partners

Enterprise Products Partners (NYSE: EPD) is one of the top players in the North American midstream energy market. The limited partnership (LP) operates more than 50,000 miles of pipelines that transport crude oil, natural gas, natural gas liquids (NGLs), and other hydrocarbon products.

This pipeline stock pays an especially juicy distribution yield of 5.5%. Enterprise Products Partners has increased its distribution for 27 consecutive years, a record that few in the midstream industry have achieved.

Story Continues

The energy sector can be highly volatile. For example, major disruptions to the sector during the last two decades include the financial crisis of 2007 through 2009, the oil price collapse of 2015 through 2017, and the COVID-19 pandemic of 2020 through 2022. But Enterprise Products Partners generated resilient cash flow throughout the period.

What does the future hold for the LP? I predict more success. Data centers hosting artificial intelligence (AI) applications require massive amounts of electric power. Natural gas is a great fit for fueling the power plants serving these data centers. The demand for U.S.-produced oil and gas is also likely to grow, which should translate to higher volumes flowing through Enterprise's pipelines.

3. NextEra Energy

NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) ranks as the largest utility company by market cap. It owns Florida Power & Light. The company is also the world leader in renewable energy and battery storage.

The utility giant's forward dividend yield stands at around 2.8%. NextEra expects the dividend to rise by roughly 10% this year, followed by an average annual growth rate of 6% through 2028. This target should be quite attainable if the company achieves its goal of compound annual growth in adjusted earnings per share of at least 8% through 2035.

The same AI tailwinds for Enterprise Products Partners should also help NextEra Energy. The company is also poised to benefit from continued population growth in Florida.

NextEra should soon be in an even stronger position to profit from AI. It plans to acquire Dominion Energy (NYSE: D) for around $66.8 billion. Dominion serves customers in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina. Virginia is the home to the world's largest data center market.

news.Title: йїГдїБеЛ≥дЄАеИ∞йїЮзЗГи°МжГЕпЉБеП∞иВ°жЬђйА±жМСжИ∞4иРђ4пЉЯ  еИЖжЮРеЄЂжХ≤и≠¶йРШпЉЪйАЩиЃКжХЄе∞ПењГ
news.Content:
иЉЭйБФпЉИNVIDIAпЉЙеЯЈи°МйХЈйїГдїБеЛ≥жШ®пЉИ23пЉЙжЧ•жПРеЙНжКµеП∞пЉМеЖНеЇ¶еЄґеЛХAIдЊЫжЗЙйПИиИЗCOMPUTEXй°МжЭРеНЗжЇЂгАВеП∞иВ°дЄКйА±еЕИйЬЗзЫ™еЊМиљЙеЉЈпЉМйА±еЫЫзЫ§дЄ≠жФєеѓЂж≠ЈеП≤жЦ∞йЂШпЉМйА±дЇФеЖНеЙµжФґзЫ§жЦ∞йЂШпЉМеК†жђКжМЗжХЄжФґеЬ®4иРђ2267йїЮпЉМеЦЃеС®дЄКжЉ≤1095йїЮпЉМжЉ≤еєЕ2.66%пЉМжЧ•еЭЗйЗПзіД1.1еЕЖеЕГпЉМеСИзПЊеГєйЗПйљКжПЪгАВжКХй°Ій†РжЄђпЉМжЬђйА±жМЗжХЄеНАйЦУзЬЛ4иРђ0500йїЮиЗ≥4иРђ4000йїЮгАВ

дЄКйА±дЇФзЫ§йЭҐй°МжЭРйЫЖдЄ≠еЬ®AIиИЗеНКе∞ОйЂФдЊЫжЗЙйПИгАВиґЕеЊЃпЉИAMDпЉЙеЃ£еЄГе∞ЗжЦ•и≥ЗйАЊзЩЊеДДзЊОеЕГжКХи≥ЗеП∞зБ£дЊЫжЗЙйПИпЉМеЄґеЛХеНКе∞ОйЂФгАБеЕИйА≤е∞Би£ЭжЧПзЊ§иљЙеЉЈпЉМеП∞з©НйЫїзµВе†ідЄКжЉ≤1.12%пЉМжФґеЬ®2255еЕГгАВеП¶дЄАжЦєйЭҐпЉМжЙЗеЗЇеЮЛйЭҐжЭњзіЪе∞Би£ЭпЉИFOPLPпЉЙй°МжЭРжМБзЇМзЩЉйЕµпЉМйЭҐжЭњйЫЩиЩОеЬ®и≤ЈзЫ§жєІеЕ•дЄЛйЫЩйЫЩдЇЃзЗИжЉ≤еБЬпЉМеЕґдЄ≠зЊ§еЙµеЦЃжЧ•жИРдЇ§йЗПз™Бз†і38иРђеЉµпЉМжИРзВЇеЄВе†ізД¶йїЮгАВ

дЄНйБОпЉМдЄЙе§Іж≥ХдЇЇжЬђйА±еЛХеРСеЗЇзПЊеИЖж≠ІгАВдЄКеС®дЄЙе§Іж≥ХдЇЇеРИи®Ии≤ЈиґЕ147.99еДДеЕГпЉМеЕґдЄ≠жКХдњ°йА£зЇМзђђ5еС®еК†зҐЉпЉМеЦЃеС®и≤ЈиґЕ502.13еДДеЕГпЉМжИРзВЇжТРзЫ§дЄїеКЫпЉЫе§Ци≥ЗеЙЗйА£зЇМзђђ2еС®и≥£иґЕпЉМйЗСй°НйБФ186.39еДДеЕГпЉМиЗ™зЗЯеХЖдєЯйА£зЇМзђђ5еС®жЄЫжМБпЉМи≥£иґЕ167.75еДДеЕГгАВжПЫи®АдєЛпЉМжЬђеЬЯи≥ЗйЗСйЫЦжМБзЇМйА≤е†іпЉМдљЖе§Ци≥ЗиИЗиЗ™зЗЯеХЖдїНеБПеРСи™њзѓАпЉМз±МзҐЉдЄ¶йЭЮеЕ®йЭҐеРМж≠•иљЙе§ЪгАВ

ж∞Єи±РжКХй°Іи°®з§ЇпЉМдЄКеС®еП∞иВ°еСИзПЊгАМVеЮЛйЬЗзЫ™гАНпЉМжИРдЇ§йЗПзґ≠жМБеЬ®1еЕЖеЕГйЩДињСпЉМй°ѓз§Їе§Ъз©ЇдЇ§жИ∞дїНзЫЄзХґжњАзГИгАВзФ±жЦЉзЫЃеЙНжМЗжХЄеЈ≤дЊЖеИ∞еЙНж≥ҐйЂШйїЮе§ІйЗПе£УеКЫеНАпЉМеН≥дљње§ЪжЦєж∞£еЛҐдїНеЉЈпЉМзЯ≠зЈЪдїНйЬАи¶БйАПйБОйЗПиГљжФЊе§ІжИЦзЫ§жХіжПЫжЙЛпЉМе∞Зе£УеКЫеНАиљЙзВЇжФѓжТРгАВж∞Єи±РжКХй°ІдєЯжМЗеЗЇпЉМеП∞иВ°дєЦйЫҐдїНе§ІпЉМеЊМзЇМдЄНжОТйЩ§дїНжЬЙдњЃж≠£и°МжГЕпЉМиЛ•еЫЮж™ФиЗ≥жЬИзЈЪзіД4иРђйїЮжФѓжТРйЩДињСпЉМеЬ®еЯЇжЬђйЭҐжЬ™еЛХжРЦдЄЛпЉМеПѓеЖНдЉЇж©ЯеЄГе±АпЉЫзЯ≠зЈЪеЃЬз≠ЙеЊЕи®КиЩЯпЉМйХЈзЈЪеЙЗзґ≠жМБеБПе§ЪзЬЛж≥ХгАВ

зОЙе±±зІСжКАе≥ґеЯЇйЗСзґУзРЖдЇЇзОЛеБЙеУ≤жМЗеЗЇпЉМеП∞иВ°дЄКйА±дЇФеЖНеЙµжФґзЫ§ж≠ЈеП≤жЦ∞йЂШпЉМеПНжШ†еЯЇжЬђйЭҐдїНеЉЈпЉМAIиґ®еЛҐдєЯжМБзЇМеЬ®иїМйБУдЄКгАВйЪ®иСЧCOMPUTEXе∞ЗжЦЉ6жЬИ2жЧ•иЗ≥5жЧ•еЬ®еНЧжЄѓе±Хи¶љй§®зЩїе†іпЉМйїГдїБеЛ≥и®™еП∞гАБгАМеЕЖеЕГеЃігАНгАБAMDеЯЈи°МйХЈиШЗеІњдЄ∞дЊЖеП∞жЉФи™™з≠ЙзІСжКАй°МжЭРпЉМй†РжЬЯе∞ЗеЖНеЇ¶еРЄеЉХзЫ§йЭҐи≥ЗйЗСиБЪзД¶AIдЊЫжЗЙйПИгАВ

еЕГе§ІжКХй°ІеЙЗжПРйЖТпЉМеП∞иВ°жХійЂФе§Ъй†≠жЮґжІЛдїНжЬ™з†іе£ЮгАВдЄНйБОпЉМжИРдЇ§еАЉиЗ™еЕИеЙНеЙµдЄЛ1еЕЖ5240еДДеЕГе§©йЗПеЊМпЉМеЈ≤зґУйБО12еАЛдЇ§жШУжЧ•жЬ™иГљеЖНз™Бз†іжЦ∞йЂШпЉМдЄФйЗПиГљйАРжЉЄиРОзЄЃпЉМй°ѓз§ЇињљеГєеКЫйБУдїНйЬАиІАеѓЯгАВ

жКХй°Ій†РдЉ∞пЉМжЬђеС®еП∞иВ°жМЗжХЄеНАйЦУзіДиРљеЬ®4иРђ0500йїЮиЗ≥4иРђ4000йїЮгАВйЪ®иСЧ5жЬИйА≤еЕ•жЬАеЊМдЄАеС®пЉМеЄВе†іе∞ЗеРМж≠•иІАеѓЯйїГдїБеЛ≥и®™еП∞и°Мз®ЛгАБCOMPUTEXеЙНи≥ЗйЗСеН°дљНгАБе§Ци≥ЗжШѓеР¶иљЙи≤ЈпЉМдї•еПКжИРдЇ§йЗПиГљеР¶йЗНжЦ∞жФЊе§ІпЉЫиЛ•йЗПиГљзД°ж≥ХйЕНеРИпЉМеП∞иВ°еН≥дљњзґ≠жМБе§Ъй†≠ж†Ље±АпЉМзЯ≠зЈЪйЬЗзЫ™еєЕеЇ¶дїНеПѓиГљеК†еКЗгАВ

news.Title: The AI Stock I'm Buying for My Retirement Portfolio -- and Why It Has Nothing to Do With Hype
news.Content:
At 35, I'm still a couple of decades from retirement. I hope all the stocks in my portfolio are worth keeping for the next 30 years and beyond. But a lot can change over that much time, so I can't be sure every company will end up part of my retirement portfolio.

I'm reasonably confident in a few of them, and the company at the top of the list is my favorite artificial intelligence (AI) stock: Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) , which owns Google. Alphabet stock has more than doubled over the last year, but I'm not buying because of hype. It's the No. 1 stock in my retirement portfolio because I think it's the best long-term value in the tech sector.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need.

Continue ¬ї

Alphabet has all the tools to win the AI race

When Alphabet stock sank below $170 last year, one of the main reasons was the concern that Google Search was vulnerable to AI. Instead, Alphabet has turned AI into a strength.

Google incorporated AI Overviews, and last July, Google CEO Sundar Pichai reported that these AI Overviews had over 2 billion users per month. There's been no indication that AI chatbots have damaged Google Search financially, either. Google Search revenue increased by 19% year over year to $60.4 billion in the first quarter of 2026.

Google also improved its own AI assistant, Gemini, making it more competitive with ChatGPT and Claude. While I still personally prefer Claude, Gemini has clearly gotten much better over the last year, and that's reflected in usage.

The Gemini app has over 750 million monthly active users. In the first quarter, Alphabet reported that its first-party models processed more than 16 billion tokens per minute via direct API use from customers, a 60% increase from the previous quarter.

What sets Alphabet apart from other top AI stocks is that it controls its entire AI stack. It has its own custom silicon, AI models, cloud computing, and a distribution layer through its existing products. This full-stack approach makes Alphabet far less reliant on other AI companies and is why I feel most confident in its long-term success.

A successful conglomerate at a reasonable price

Google Search is Alphabet's biggest revenue driver, but the company has several successful businesses. Google Cloud made $20 billion in sales in Q1 2026, a 63% year-over-year increase, and its backlog nearly doubled from the previous quarter to over $460 billion.

Story Continues

It also owns YouTube, and while ad revenue from that is a solid $9.9 billion, another benefit is that it gets users to sign up for premium plans. Pichai said that YouTube is a key driver behind Google's 350 million paid subscriptions, which doesn't surprise me. YouTube Premium is easily the subscription service I use the most and the last one I'd want to cut.

Another business worth mentioning is Waymo, Alphabet's autonomous driving technology company. It surpassed 500,000 paid robotaxi rides per week in the first quarter, and after a $16 billion investment round, it's now valued at $126 billion.

Because Alphabet's share price has increased so much, the question often arises: Is it still a good investment? The valuation actually isn't particularly expensive, especially for one of the leading tech stocks . Alphabet trades at 27 times forward earnings, comparable to Nvidia at 26 times forward earnings. Its trailing price/earnings-to-growth (PEG) ratio is below 0.7, indicating it's undervalued relative to its earnings growth.

What about the risks?

The biggest worry with Alphabet is the same one investors have about all the major hyperscalers: massive capital expenditures (capex). In its first-quarter earnings call, Alphabet raised capex guidance for 2026 to between $180 billion and $190 billion.

This has become par for the course with companies building AI data centers. Combined, the four major hyperscalers (Alphabet, Amazon , Meta Platforms , and Microsoft ) forecast $600 billion to $700 billion in capex spending in 2026, according to recent research by The Motley Fool.

Even for a profitable business like Alphabet, this kind of spending will drain its free cash flow ($64.4 billion over the trailing 12 months). If revenue from AI products and services falls short of expectations, the company's spending could prove a costly mistake.

To be honest, I'm not worried about Alphabet's capex spending. Demand for computing power is skyrocketing, and AI data centers can become another source of revenue. In April, Anthropic signed a partnership with Google and Broadcom for computing capacity, and earlier this month, it committed to spending $200 billion with Google Cloud over five years.

No company is a sure investment. But with its AI-powered growth and all the successful businesses under its umbrella, Alphabet is the one I trust the most to continue compounding over the next three decades.

news.Title: йїГдїБеЛ≥жПРеЙНдЊЖеП∞пЉБжЫњCOMPUTEXжЪЦиЇЂйЗНзЗГAIзЖ±гААеП∞иВ°еЄГе±АдЇЃйїЮдЄАжђ°зЬЛ
news.Content:
гАРи®ШиАЕеСВжЙњеУ≤пЉПеП∞еМЧе†±е∞ОгАСиЉЭйБФпЉИNVIDIAпЉЙеЯЈи°МйХЈйїГдїБеЛ≥жПРеЙНдЊЖеП∞пЉМеЄВе†ідєЯжПРеЙНйїЮзЗГCOMPUTEXиИЗAIдЊЫжЗЙйПИи°МжГЕгАВйЗОжЭСжКХдњ°и°®з§ЇпЉМйЫЦзДґ2026еєіеЕ®зРГзґУжњЯжИРйХЈеЛХиГљиЉГеЙНеєЊеєізХ•зВЇжФЊзЈ©пЉМдљЖеЬ®дЄїи¶БзґУжњЯйЂФдїНеЕЈйЯМжАІжФѓжТРдЄЛпЉМжХійЂФжЩѓж∞£зґ≠жМБгАМжФЊзЈ©дљЖз©©еБ•гАНж†Ље±АпЉМAIдїНжШѓдїКеєіжЬАж†ЄењГзЪДжИРйХЈдЄїиїЄгАВйЪ®иСЧеЬЛйЪЫзІСжКАе§Іеї†жМБзЇМжУіе§ІAIи≥ЗжЬђжФѓеЗЇпЉМеК†дЄКCOMPUTEXеН≥е∞ЗзЩїе†іпЉМAIдЊЫжЗЙйПИзЖ±еЇ¶жЬЙжЬЫеїґзЇМиЗ≥дЄЛеНКеєігАВ

йЗОжЭСжКХдњ°жКХи≥Зз≠ЦзХ•йГ®еЙѓзЄљзґУзРЖж®УеЕЛжЬЫи°®з§ЇпЉМзЫЃеЙНеЄВе†ідїНйЂШеЇ¶йЧЬж≥®йАЪиЖ®иИЗеИ©зОЗиµ∞еЛҐпЉМе∞§еЕґдЄ≠жЭ±еЬ∞зЈ£жФњж≤їи°Эз™БжМБзЇМпЉМдљњиГљжЇРдЊЫзµ¶дЄНзҐЇеЃЪжАІеНЗйЂШпЉМж≤єеГєзґ≠жМБйЂШж™ФйЬЗзЫ™пЉМдєЯжО®еНЗйАЪиЖ®йїПиСЧеЇ¶пЉМиЃУиБѓжЇЦжЬГпЉИFedпЉЙе∞НйЩНжБѓжЕЛеЇ¶жЫіиґ®еѓ©жЕОгАВеЬ®ж≠§зТ∞еҐГдЄЛпЉМйЗСиЮНеЄВе†ізЯ≠жЬЯж≥ҐеЛХдїНйЫ£йБњеЕНпЉМдљЖдЄ¶жЬ™жФєиЃКдЄ≠йХЈжЬЯжИРйХЈиґ®еЛҐгАВ

ж®УеЕЛжЬЫжМЗеЗЇпЉМAIдїНжШѓ2026еєіжЬАйЗНи¶БзЪДзФҐж•≠еЛХиГљгАВеЕ®зРГйЫ≤зЂѓжЬНеЛЩеХЖиИЗзІСжКАйЊНй†≠жМБзЇМжУіе§ІAIжКХи≥ЗпЉМеЊЮж®°еЮЛи®УзЈіеИ∞жО®иЂЦйЬАж±ВеРМж≠•еҐЮеК†пЉМеЄґеЛХAIдЉЇжЬНеЩ®гАБйЂШйАЯйБЛзЃЧиИЗи≥ЗжЦЩдЄ≠ењГеїЇи®≠йЬАж±ВжМБзЇМеНЗжЇЂгАВе∞§еЕґеП∞з©НйЫїеЕИеЙНж≥Хи™™жЬГйЗЛеЗЇж≠£еРСе±ХжЬЫеЊМпЉМдєЯйА≤дЄАж≠•зҐЇи™НAIзЫЄйЧЬйЬАж±ВдїНеЕЈеЉЈеЛБеЛХиГљпЉМй†РжЬЯзЫЄйЧЬжКХи≥Зе∞ЗеїґзЇМиЗ≥2026еєідЄЛеНКеєігАВ

йЗОжЭСйіїйБЛеЯЇйЗСеПКйЗОжЭСдЄ≠е∞ПеЯЇйЗСзґУзРЖдЇЇйВ±зњ†жђ£и°®з§ЇпЉМеП∞зБ£еЬ®еЕ®зРГAIдЊЫжЗЙйПИдЄ≠жЙЃжЉФйЧЬйНµиІТиЙ≤пЉМжґµиУЛжЩґеЬУдї£еЈ•гАБеЕИйА≤е∞Би£ЭгАБAIдЉЇжЬНеЩ®зµДи£ЭиИЗйЧЬйНµйЫґзµДдїґз≠Йй†ШеЯЯпЉМеЕЈеВЩйЂШеЇ¶зЂґзИ≠еД™еЛҐгАВеЬ®AIйЬАж±ВжМБзЇМжУіеЉµдЄЛпЉМзЫЄйЧЬдЉБж•≠зЗЯйБЛиГљи¶ЛеЇ¶иИЗзН≤еИ©жМБзЇМдЄКдњЃпЉМдєЯжИРзВЇжФѓжТРиВ°еГєзЪДйЗНи¶БеЯЇжЬђйЭҐгАВ

йВ±зњ†жђ£и™НзВЇпЉМеН≥дљњзЯ≠жЬЯеЫ†и©ХеГєйЭҐжИЦи≥ЗйЗСиЉ™еЛХе∞ОиЗіиВ°еГєж≥ҐеЛХпЉМдљЖеЬ®зН≤еИ©жИРйХЈиґ®еЛҐжЬ™жФєиЃКжГЕж≥БдЄЛпЉМжЛЙеЫЮдїНеЕЈеЄГе±АеГєеАЉгАВ

йЗЭе∞НеН≥е∞ЗзЩїе†ізЪД2026еєіCOMPUTEXпЉМйВ±зњ†жђ£жМЗеЗЇпЉМдїКеєідЄїй°МеЃЪи™њзВЇгАМAI TogetherгАНпЉМиБЪзД¶AIеЯЇз§ОеїЇи®≠иИЗжЗЙзФ®иРљеЬ∞пЉМй†РжЬЯеЊЮжЩґзЙЗйБЛзЃЧиГљеКЫгАБдЉЇжЬНеЩ®жЮґжІЛеИ∞зµВзЂѓжЗЙзФ®пЉМйГље∞ЗеЃМжХіе±ХзПЊAIзФЯжЕЛз≥їзЩЉе±ХжЦєеРСгАВеЄВе†ідєЯй†РжЬЯпЉМеЬЛйЪЫе§Іеї†еЬ®е±ХжЬГжЬЯйЦУеЕђеЄГзЪДжЦ∞зФҐеУБиИЗз≠ЦзХ•еЄГе±АпЉМе∞ЗйА≤дЄАж≠•жПРеНЗеЄВе†іе∞НAIйХЈзЈЪжИРйХЈзЪДдњ°ењГгАВ

йВ±зњ†жђ£еИЖжЮРпЉМзЫЃеЙНжЬАеАЉеЊЧйЧЬж≥®зЪДAIдЊЫжЗЙйПИжЧПзЊ§пЉМеМЕжЛђеЕИйА≤и£љз®ЛиИЗе∞Би£ЭгАБйЂШйАЯеВ≥иЉЄгАБжХ£зЖ±иИЗйЫїеКЫзЃ°зРЖпЉМдї•еПКеЕЈдЊЫйЬАеД™еЛҐзЪДдЄКжЄЄжЭРжЦЩдЊЫжЗЙеХЖгАВйАЩдЇЫй†ШеЯЯдЄНеГЕзЫіжО•еПЧжГ†AIзЃЧеКЫйЬАж±ВжПРеНЗпЉМеРМжЩВдєЯеЫ†жКАи°УиИЗзФҐиГљйЦАж™їиЉГйЂШпЉМеЕЈеВЩиЉГдљ≥еГєж†ЉиИЗзН≤еИ©жФѓжТРиГљеКЫгАВ

йЗОжЭСжКХдњ°и™НзВЇпЉМеЬ®AIйХЈзЈЪжИРйХЈиґ®еЛҐжЬ™иЃКгАБи≥ЗйЗСжМБзЇМеЫЮжµБпЉМдї•еПКеЬЛйЪЫзІСжКАе§Іеї†жМБзЇМйЗЛеЗЇж≠£еРСе±ХжЬЫдЄЛпЉМеП∞иВ°еЊМеЄВдїНжЬЙж©ЯжЬГзґ≠жМБеБПе§Ъж†Ље±АгАВйЫЦзДґзЯ≠жЬЯеПѓиГљеЫ†еЄВе†іжГЕзЈТиИЗи©ХеГєйЭҐеЗЇзПЊйЬЗзЫ™пЉМдљЖеЬ®еЯЇжЬђйЭҐжФѓжТРдЄЛпЉМжЛЙеЫЮдїНеПѓи¶ЦзВЇдЄ≠йХЈжЬЯеЄГе±АAIдЄїй°МзЪДйЗНи¶Бж©ЯжЬГгАВ

news.Title: The Gates Foundation Just Sold All of Its Microsoft Stock. Is It Time to Panic?
news.Content:
Bill Gates and Paul Allen founded Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) in 1975 and built one of the world's most dominant companies. While Microsoft has evolved throughout the years, Gates stayed involved until 2020, when he left the board of directors.

One of Gates' biggest legacies has been the Gates Foundation. This foundation has a trust that holds several major companies, but it made a jaw-dropping move during the first quarter: It sold all of its Microsoft stock.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

That obviously raises some red flags, but should investors start to panic?

The fund is scheduled to shut down by 2045

One item to note is that the foundation is scheduled to shut down by 2045, so it must donate all of its funds by then. So, eventually, it will need to sell all of its holdings. While the fund completely sold out of Microsoft during Q1, it also sold other staples, like Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA) (NYSE: BRKB) .

As a result, the selling doesn't look so bad, but the timing certainly does. Microsoft has been struggling lately, and while most of the big tech companies have hit new all-time highs, Microsoft is down more than 20% from its recent highs. It has also reached a valuation low point that it hasn't seen in some time.

Using the cash from operations metric to value a stock is useful when a company is going through a heavy capital-investment cycle, like Microsoft is with artificial intelligence (AI). This metric measures how much cash a business can produce, and I think it's the perfect metric to use with big tech companies right now. From this measure, Microsoft hasn't been this cheap since 2019. That's a huge retraction, and if the Gates Foundation believes this is the new normal, it could be a smart move to sell.

However, I don't think that's the case.

Microsoft is in a great position to capitalize on the massive amount of AI spending going on, and its cloud computing platform, Azure, is becoming one of the best places to build and run AI models. This is already leading to strong short-term growth, and it will likely translate into major long-term growth as these massive capital investments turn into cash cows .

I think the Gates Foundation Trust selling Microsoft stock is more noise than signal, as Gates really has no involvement with the company. So, just because he founded Microsoft doesn't mean you need to follow his fund's move .

Story Continues

news.Title: 3 Monster Stocks to Hold for the Next 20 Years
news.Content:
"Monster stock" is usually shorthand for a company that has compounded at exceptional rates for years, and still has plenty of runway left. It's the kind of name you regret for not owning earlier: think Tesla or Nvidia .

Today, three consumer-facing companies fit that mold across very different parts of the wallet, and each one holds up when you project the business 20 years out -- even if you missed the early innings.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї

1. Amazon

Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) is the most obvious of the three and the easiest to underestimate because it has been a market darling for so long that investors assume the growth math no longer works. Its first-quarter 2026 results suggest otherwise. AWS revenue grew 28% year over year to $37.6 billion (the fastest AWS growth in 15 quarters); the chips business surpassed a $20 billion revenue run rate growing in triple digits; advertising trailing-12-month revenue exceeded $70 billion, and stores unit growth hit 15%, the highest since the late stages of pandemic-era demand.

The 20-year case for Amazon is not really about retail or even cloud in isolation. It is about a company that has built four distinct, high-margin growth engines -- Amazon Web Services, advertising, custom silicon, and (more recently) healthcare and media -- on top of a retail base that throws off cash. Each new engine starts small and becomes meaningful, which is the pattern long-term compounders tend to follow.

The risk with Amazon is regulatory. Amazon's scale means that antitrust action is a recurring possibility, and any forced separation of AWS from the retail and advertising businesses would be a significant event.

2. O'Reilly Automotive

O'Reilly Automotive (NASDAQ: ORLY) is the dullest-sounding of these three, which is part of why it works. The company sells auto parts to both do-it-yourself and professional repair customers, and the underlying U.S. car parc (the number and average age of vehicles on the road) is one of the most reliable demand drivers in retail. In the first quarter of 2026, O'Reilly raised full-year 2026 guidance for total revenue of between $18.7 billion and $19 billion, comparable store sales growth of 3% to 5%, and diluted EPS between $3.15 and $3.25.

The 20-year case for me rests on three structural facts. Cars are getting older on average, which increases parts demand. Professional repair shops continue to gain share at the expense of dealer-only service, which favors O'Reilly's dual-market strategy. And the company has been one of the most aggressive and disciplined buyers of its own stock for years, which means earnings per share grow even faster than total earnings.

Story Continues

The risk for me regarding O'Reilly is electric vehicles and self-driving cars. Battery-electric cars have fewer wear-and-tear parts than internal combustion engines, and if the U.S. fleet shifts decisively to electric over the next two decades, the total addressable market for traditional aftermarket parts will eventually compress.

3. Nike

Nike (NYSE: NKE) fits the "monster stock" profile as a global consumer franchise with unparalleled brand equity and distribution, even as it works through a difficult stretch. In fiscal Q3 2026, revenue was roughly flat, while earnings per share fell down 35% year over year as higher tariffs, promotions, and inventory cleanвАСup weighed on margins.

The long-term case is simpler: Nike remains the largest sneaker brand globally, and it still generates more than $50 billion (in 2024) in annual revenue across footwear, apparel, and equipment. Over a 20-year horizon, a combination of scale, marketing muscle, and product innovation should matter more than the current margin reset.

Why these three work as a combined position

The three names cover different sub-categories of consumer behavior -- discretionary retail and digital services (Amazon), branded footwear (Nike), and necessity-based vehicle maintenance (O'Reilly). The correlation across them is lower than it looks, which makes the combined position more resilient than any one of them alone.

All three also share the characteristics that tend to predict 20-year outperformance: clear category leadership, demonstrated ability to grow at scale, disciplined capital allocation, and management teams that have generally taken the long view on reinvestment.

news.Title: й©ЪеВ≥еП∞з©НйЫїе∞Зз†Н15%еИЖзіЕпЉЯгААеУ°еЈ•жПЪи®АпЉЪдЄНжОТйЩ§жХИж≥ХдЄЙжШЯгАМзљЈеЈ•гАН

еП∞з©НйЫїдїКпЉИ2026пЉЙеєіиВ°еГєи°®зПЊдЇЃзЬЉпЉМзђђдЄАе≠£еИ©жљ§иЉГеОїеєіеРМжЬЯеҐЮйХЈдЇЖ58%пЉМеЙµдЄЛж≠ЈеП≤жЦ∞йЂШгАВдљЖиЗЙжЫЄз§ЊеЬШгАМTSMCе§Іе∞ПдЇЛгАНињСжЧ•еНїзИЖеЗЇйЬЗжТЉељИпЉМе§ІжЙєеУ°еЈ•дЄНжїњеЕђеПЄзІїе∞Зз†Н15%еИЖзіЕпЉМжАТжЙєйЂШе±§жѓЂзД°и™†дњ°пЉМзКІзЙ≤еУ°еЈ•еИ©зЫКи®Ое•љиВ°жЭ±гАВжЬЙйССжЦЉеНЧйЯУдЄЙжШЯйЫїе≠РеЈ•жЬГзљЈеЈ•зµРжЮЬе∞ЗжЦЉжЬђпЉИ5пЉЙжЬИ27жЧ•еЗЇзИРпЉМйГ®еИЖдЄНжїњзЪДеП∞з©НйЫїеУ°еЈ•жПЪи®АпЉМдЄНжОТйЩ§жХИж≥ХдЄЙжШЯеУ°еЈ•гАМзљЈеЈ•гАНгАВиЗ≥жЦЉеП∞з©НйЫїдїКеєіеЇ¶зҐЇеИЗеИЖзіЕжФњз≠ЦпЉМе∞ЪжЬЙеЊЕеП∞з©НйЫїеЃШжЦєеЗЇйЭҐи™™жШОгАВ

еП∞з©НйЫїи°®зПЊдЇЃзЬЉ еНїй©ЪеВ≥з†НеУ°еЈ•15%еИЖзіЕпЉЯ

зІСжКАе™ТйЂФгАКWccftechгАЛе†±е∞ОжМЗеЗЇпЉМеП∞з©НйЫїеЬ®еП∞зБ£зЪДзФҐж•≠зґУжњЯдЄ≠дљФжЬЙзЙєжЃКеЬ∞дљНпЉМеЄЄиҐЂи≠љзВЇгАМи≠ЈеЬЛз•Юе±±гАНпЉМдЄФеЬ®дЇЇеЈ•жЩЇжЕІпЉИAIпЉЙзЫЄйЧЬйБЛзЃЧйЬАж±ВиУђеЛГзЩЉе±ХзЪДиГМжЩѓдЄЛпЉМеП∞з©НйЫїдїКеєіиВ°еГєи°®зПЊдЇЃзЬЉпЉМзђђдЄАе≠£еИ©жљ§иЉГеОїеєіеРМжЬЯеҐЮйХЈдЇЖ58%пЉМеЙµдЄЛж≠ЈеП≤жЦ∞йЂШгАВ

дљЖеП¶жЬЙжґИжБѓз®±пЉМеП∞з©НйЫїжУђз†НеУ°еЈ•еИЖзіЕйЂШйБФ15%пЉМеУ°еЈ•еЫ†иАМеЬ®иЗЙжЫЄз§ЊеЬШжЖ§жАТзЩЉиБ≤пЉМжФЊи©±дЄНжОТйЩ§жХИж≥ХдЄЙжШЯйЫїе≠РеУ°еЈ•йА≤и°МгАМе§ІзљЈеЈ•гАНгАВеЕґдЄ≠жЬЙеУ°еЈ•жК±жА®йЂШе±§гАМжГ≥жФєдїАйЇЉе∞±жФєдїАйЇЉпЉМжГ≥дїАйЇЉжЩВеАЩжФєе∞±дїАйЇЉжЩВеАЩжФєпЉМе∞±еГПдїЦеАСзЪДеЕІйГ®зЃ°зРЖйҐ®ж†ЉдЄАж®£пЉМжѓЂзД°дњ°и≠љеПѓи®АгАНпЉЫеП¶жЬЙеУ°еЈ•жАТеЦКпЉМгАМжИСеАСжѓПе§©жЙњеПЧиСЧеЈ®е§ІзЪДе£УеКЫпЉМзВЇеЕђеПЄзКІзЙ≤иЗ™еЈ±зЪДзФЯжіїпЉМзПЊеЬ®еЕђеПЄеНїжГ≥еЙКжЄЫеУ°еЈ•зНОйЗСдЊЖеЫЮе†±иВ°жЭ±пЉЯгАН

еП∞з©НйЫїзВЇзґ≠жМБжКАи°Уй†ШеЕИ и≥ЗжЬђжФѓеЗЇйЊРе§ІеПѓиГље∞ОиЗіеЙКжЄЫеИЖзіЕ

и©≤е†±е∞ОдєЯжМЗеЗЇпЉМеП∞з©НйЫїдЉЉдєОзД°жДПеЬ®зФҐиГљеТМжКАи°Уй†Ше∞ОеЬ∞дљНдЄКиЃУж≠•пЉМзЫЃеЙНжЬЙйЂШйБФ12еЇІдЄНеРМзЪДжЩґеЬУеї†ж≠£иЩХжЦЉеїЇи®≠зЪДдЄНеРМйЪОжЃµпЉМдї•йЮПеЫЇеЕґеЬ®2е•Из±≥еТМA14пЉИ1.4е•Из±≥пЉЙи£љз®ЛзѓАйїЮдЄКзЪДй†ШеЕИеЬ∞дљНгАВе¶Вж≠§йЊРе§ІзЪДи≥ЗжЬђжФѓеЗЇпЉМеПѓиГљж≠£жШѓеП∞з©НйЫїж≠£еЬ®иАГжЕЃеЙКжЄЫиИЗеУ°еЈ•зЫЄйЧЬзЪДйЦЛжФѓзЪДеОЯеЫ†гАВ

зљЈеЈ•йҐ®йЪ™йБ†йЂШдЄЙжШЯ еЕђеПЄйЂШе±§иЃУж≠•еПѓиГљжАІйЂШ

зДґиАМпЉМеП∞з©НйЫїеУ°еЈ•зљЈеЈ•зЪДйҐ®йЪ™йБ†йЂШжЦЉдЄЙжШЯгАВзХҐзЂЯпЉМеП∞з©НйЫїжШѓAIеїЇи®≠зЪДж†ЄењГпЉМеАШдљњж≤ТжЬЙеП∞з©НйЫїжЩґеЬУеї†е§Іи¶Пж®°зФЯзФҐзЪДAIжЩґзЙЗеТМGPUпЉМеП∞зБ£зЪДAIжЈШйЗСзЖ±еєЊдєОиВѓеЃЪжЬГеі©жљ∞пЉМжЫіеПѓиГљеЄґиµ∞жХЄеЕЖзЊОеЕГзЪДжКХи≥ЗгАВи©≤е†±е∞ОеЫ†иАМзМЬжЄђпЉМиЛ•еП∞з©НйЫїеУ°еЈ•зИЖзЩЉиИЗдЄЙжШЯйЫїе≠РйЫЈеРМзЪДзљЈеЈ•иЈ°и±°пЉМеЕђеПЄйЂШе±§еЊИеПѓиГљжЬГеБЪеЗЇзЫЄжЗЙиЃУж≠•гАВ

news.Title: Here's the One Thing New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Could Do That Would Actually Crash the Stock Market
news.Content: When Jerome Powell served as Federal Reserve chair, the central bank's policy decisions were largely predictable. Powell typically telegraphed in advance what changes were likely to come. He answered questions in press conferences in a way that, at least for the most part, reassured markets. But Powell is no longer at the helm of the Federal Reserve. He passed the baton to President Trump's nominee, Kevin Warsh, on May 22, 2026. Warsh has called for "regime change" at the Fed. Uncertainty about what actions he might take has caused some investors to worry. Many of those concerns are ...

вАҐ Concerns are raised about Warsh's less communicative approach and potential shifts in inflation metrics or balance sheet reduction.
вАҐ A significant market crash could occur if Warsh undermines the Fed's political independence by appeasing political pressures.

news.Keywords: Federal Reserve, monetary policy, interest rates, inflation, stock market crash

ињСеєіжИњеГєиИЗиВ°еЄВеРМж≠•й£ЖжЉ≤пЉМдЄНе∞СдЇЇдєЯйЦЛеІЛзМґи±ЂгАМеИ∞еЇХи©≤и≤ЈжИњйВДжШѓи≤ЈиВ°гАНгАВжЬЙдЇЇи™НзВЇжИњи≤Єе£УеКЫж≤ЙйЗНпЉМдЄНе¶ВзЫіжО•жКХеЕ•иВ°еЄВжЛЪе†±йЕђпЉЫдљЖдєЯжЬЙдЇЇйЭ†иСЧжИњеГєдЄКжЉ≤иИЗи≥ЗзФҐжІУж°њпЉМжИРеКЯиЃУи≤°еѓМењЂйАЯзњїеАНгАВињСжЧ•пЉМжЬЙзґ≤еПЛзЩЉжЦЗи°®з§ЇпЉМиЗ™еЈ±зХґеєізВЇдЇЖйАЉиЗ™еЈ±е≠ШйМҐиАМи≤ЈжИњпЉМж≤ТжГ≥еИ∞еЊМдЊЖзЬЛеИ∞еП∞иВ°дЄАиЈѓеЙµйЂШпЉМеПНиАМйЦЛеІЛжЗЈзЦСгАМзХґеИЭжШѓдЄНжШѓдЄНи©≤и≤ЈжИњгАНпЉМи≤ЉжЦЗжЫЭеЕЙеЊМзЂЛеИїеЉХзЩЉзЖ±и≠∞гАВ

дЄАеРНзґ≤еПЛжЧ•еЙНеЬ® Dcard дЄКдї•гАМеЊМжВФи≤ЈжИњеЧОгАНзВЇй°МзЩЉжЦЗпЉМжМЗеЗЇиЗ™еЈ±зХґеИЭиІ£жОЙзЊОйЗСеЃЪе≠ШпЉМеК†дЄКжЙАжЬЙе≠ШжђЊпЉМе•љдЄНеЃєжШУжєКеЗЇ200е§ЪиРђеЕГй†≠жЬЯжђЊи≤ЈжИњпЉМзЫЃеЙНеЙЗе∞ЗжИње≠РеЗЇзІЯпЉМдЄНйБОжИњзІЯжФґеЕ•еГЕиГљи≤†жУФзіД4еИЖдєЛ3зЪДжИњи≤ЄгАВдєЛеЊМе•єеПИи©ХдЉ∞иЗ™иЇЂи≤°еЛЩзЛАж≥БиИЗ2.8%зЪДеҐЮи≤ЄеИ©зОЗпЉМеЖНеАЯдЇЖ100иРђеЕГжКХеЕ•еЕГе§ІеП∞зБ£50пЉИ0050пЉЙиИЗзЊОеВµпЉМдљЖеЫ†дЄАйЦЛеІЛж≤ТжЬЙеЃИе•љжУНдљЬзіАеЊЛпЉМзЫЃеЙНеЄ≥дЄКе†±йЕђеГЕзіД25%гАВ

еОЯPOеЭ¶и®АпЉМйЫЦзДґзЫЃеЙНжХійЂФи≤°еЛЩзЛАж≥БдЄНиЗ≥жЦЉе§±и°°пЉМдљЖзЬЛеИ∞еП∞иВ°дЄАиЈѓи°ЭйЂШпЉМзЄљи¶ЇеЊЧиЗ™еЈ±йМѓйБОеЄВе†іжЬАе§ІдЄАж≥Ґи°МжГЕпЉМдєЯйЦЛеІЛжЗЈзЦСжИње≠РзЪДе†±йЕђзОЗжШѓеР¶зЬЯзЪДзђ¶еРИжИРжЬђпЉМзЫіи®АгАМе¶ВжЮЬзХґеИЭж≤ТжЬЙи≤ЈжИњжШѓдЄНжШѓжѓФиЉГе•љгАНгАВ

и≤ЉжЦЗжЫЭеЕЙеЊМзЂЛеИїеЉХиµЈи®ОиЂЦпЉМдЄНе∞СдЇЇе∞Нж≠§еЊИжЬЙеЕ±й≥іпЉМгАМж≤ТдЇЛе¶≥дєЯдЄНжШѓеФѓдЄАдЄАеАЛпЉМеЫ†зВЇжИСдєЯжШѓеЬ®е§Іе§Ъй†≠еЙНз∞љзіДиАМйМѓйБОйАЩж≥Ґи°МжГЕзЪДдЇЇгАНгАБгАМ2024и≥£иВ°и≤ЈжИњвА¶зДґеЊМеЙЫз∞љзіДе∞±йБЗйЩРи≤Єдї§пЉМжђЊй†ЕйВДеЈЃйїЮдЄЛдЄНдЊЖпЉМдЇЇзФЯеХКгАНгАБгАМжИСиЈЯдљ†зЪДжГЕж≥БдЄАж®£и™ТпЉБеП™жШѓжИСеП¶е§ЦеАЯйМҐжКХи≥ЗеЕґдїЦйГљдЄАж®°дЄАж®£пЉМжЧ©зЯ•йБУжИСе∞±дЄНи≤ЈжИњдЇЖгАНгАБгАМжИСдєЯжШѓи≤ЈжИњзЪДз§ЊзХЬпЉМзЬЛиСЧжЬЛеПЛдЄАеАЛеАЛзВТиВ°ењГжГЕдєЯи†їдљОиРљзЪДгАНгАВ

дЄНйБОдєЯжЬЙдЇЇи™НзВЇпЉМи≤ЈжИњиИЗи≤ЈиВ°еЕґеѓ¶жЬђдЊЖе∞±дЄНжШѓдЇТжЦ•йБЄй†ЕпЉМгАМи¶ЇеЊЧдЄНзФ®жГ≥е§™е§ЪжђЄпЉМжИСи≤ЈжИње∞±зХґдљЬжШѓдЄАз®Ѓз©©еЃЪеЮЛзЪДи≥ЗзФҐйЕНзљЃпЉМиВ°еЄВи≥ЇйМҐдєЯи¶Бе≠ЄжЬГдЄЛиїКж≠ҐзЫИпЉМдЄНзДґйГљжШѓзіЩдЄКеѓМи≤ігАНгАБгАМи≥ЗзФҐйЕНзљЃйШњпЉМи™∞и™™еЕ®йГ®йГљи¶БеЬ®иВ°еЄВгАНгАБгАМж≤ТдЇЛеХ¶иЗ≥е∞СдњЭеЇХйВДжЬЙжИњпЉМиВ°еЄВдєЯжЬЙйА≤е†ізН≤еИ©дЇЖгАНгАВ

вАҐ Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) is highlighted as a critical player in the AI infrastructure market, benefiting from demand for advanced logic chips.

news.Title: Nvidia's Board Just Authorized an Additional $80 Billion Buyback. Here's What That Really Signals to Investors.
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) rarely has to remind anyone how fast it's growing. So it was a little surprising when, earlier this week, the chipmaker paired its latest quarterly report with a move that looked borrowed from a mature blue chip: its board approved an additional $80 billion for share repurchases and lifted the quarterly dividend from $0.01 to $0.25 a share -- a 25-fold increase. The backdrop is what makes it notable. This is a business that just grew revenue 85% ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Nvidia announced an additional $80 billion share buyback authorization and a 25-fold increase in its quarterly dividend.
вАҐ The company reported Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6 billion, an 85% year-over-year increase, with data center revenue up 92% to $75.2 billion.
вАҐ Free cash flow reached $48.6 billion in Q1 FY2027, nearly double the previous year, supporting shareholder returns.
вАҐ Nvidia's guidance projects Q2 revenue of approximately $91 billion, indicating further acceleration.
вАҐ Risks include the loss of the Chinese market (no data center chips shipped in Q1) and increasing competition from customers designing their own AI chips.
news.Keywords: share buyback, dividend increase, revenue acceleration, free cash flow, data center growth

news.Title: Wall Street Was Sleeping on the "Bits-to-Atoms" Trade. This Growth Stock Could Profit.
news.Content: The tech sector is known for the dominance of software and digital platforms. This focus on computer "bits" enabled companies in the industry to build and distribute their offerings efficiently and cheaply, generating robust margins. However, the arrival of artificial intelligence (AI) has led to a twist in this story. Tech businesses are now increasingly ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ The tech industry is shifting from 'bits' (software) to 'atoms' (capital-intensive physical assets like data centers) due to AI.
вАҐ Nvidia partnered with Corning to expand optical connectivity manufacturing capacity tenfold, signaling a move from copper wiring to light-based data transmission.
вАҐ This partnership is driven by the need for faster data transmission in AI systems, with the AI infrastructure market projected to grow significantly.
вАҐ Corning's optical solutions are crucial for connecting components in large AI systems like Nvidia's Vera Rubin AI system.

news.Keywords: bits-to-atoms, AI infrastructure, optical connectivity, data center upgrade, semiconductor partnership

news.Title: GDP Alert: The United Kingdom Is Poorer Than Every Individual U.S. State

news.Title: WeвАЩre Not in a Bubble. Wall Street Just HasnвАЩt Caught Up With the New вАШPhysicsвАЩ of the Stock Market.
news.Content: Modern financial markets are triggering cognitive dissonance among investors. On the one hand, we're seeing historical highs among stock indices. On the other hand, there are obvious signs of macroeconomic fatigue. As I detailed recently, looking at the valuation of inflation (through the prism of the Big Mac Index), the real U.S. economy, measured in physical base goods, has actually been in a hidden recession for the last 20 years. The stock market, however, has managed to more than double over this time. More News from Barchart Microsoft vs. Apple Stock: The Numbers Reveal a Clear Winner Heading Into H2 ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ The article argues that modern financial markets are not in a bubble but represent a fundamental shift in economic structure, moving from industrial to digital.
вАҐ Traditional valuation metrics like the Buffett Indicator (228%) and Big Mac Index are misaligned with the digital economy's realities.
вАҐ The stock market's capitalization is increasingly driven by future earnings potential, particularly from companies with zero marginal costs in software and digital services.
вАҐ Companies like Microsoft and Alphabet exhibit significantly higher net profit margins (exceeding 25-40%) compared to traditional industrial companies (around 10%).
вАҐ The decoupling of stock indices from macroeconomic indicators like GDP is attributed to the revolution in profitability enabled by digital technologies.
news.Keywords: market valuation, economic shift, digital economy, zero marginal cost, profitability

news.Title: CoreWeave vs. Nebius: Which Artificial Intelligence (AI) Infrastructure Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?

news.AI_Summary:

вАҐ CoreWeave's strategy relies heavily on large-scale partnerships, with Microsoft accounting for approximately 67% of its 2025 revenue, posing a significant customer concentration risk.
вАҐ Nebius Group reported fiscal 2025 revenue of approximately $529.8 million, a 479% YoY growth, and achieved a net income of $101.7 million with a positive net margin of 19.2%.
вАҐ Nebius Group has a more diversified business strategy, serving various industries across six global regions, and shows a stronger financial position with a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.1x and a current ratio of 3.1x.
вАҐ Both companies experienced negative free cash flow due to heavy investments in data center assets, with CoreWeave at negative $7.3 billion and Nebius at negative $3.7 billion.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, cloud provider, revenue growth, profitability, customer concentration

news.Title: Prediction: Alphabet Will Join the $5 Trillion Club and the Dow Jones Industrial Average in June
news.Content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) will celebrate its 130th anniversary on May 26. But the historic index looks nothing like it did over a century ago. The Dow of today is chock-full of tech-focused companies, with Salesforce swapping with ExxonMobil in 2020 , Amazon trading places with Walgreens Boots Alliance in 2024, and Nvidia taking Intel 's spot in 2024. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Alphabet (GOOGL) is predicted to join the Dow Jones Industrial Average in June, potentially reaching a $5 trillion market cap.
вАҐ The Dow's composition has shifted towards tech, with recent additions like Amazon and Nvidia.
вАҐ Alphabet's stock split in 2022 and its diverse AI, cloud, and media offerings make it a strong candidate for Dow inclusion.
вАҐ Honeywell's upcoming spin-offs may create an opening for Alphabet in the price-weighted index.
вАҐ The article also mentions a report on a company providing critical technology for Nvidia and Intel.
news.Keywords: Dow Jones inclusion, market capitalization, AI exposure, stock split catalyst, technology sector weighting

news.Title: Billionaire Bill Ackman Just Dumped Alphabet Stock to Buy These Two AI Stocks. Should Investors Follow Suit?
news.Content: Bill Ackman is one of the world's preeminent investors. He runs a very concentrated portfolio of just a handful of stocks, so when he makes moves, investors tend to pay attention. In Q1, Ackman's Pershing Square Management (NYSE: PS) fund took a huge new stake in Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) while increasing its position in Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) by 19%. To help fund these new purchases, Ackman sold 95% of his stake in fellow ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Billionaire Bill Ackman's Pershing Square fund significantly increased its stake in Microsoft (MSFT) and Amazon (AMZN) in Q1.
вАҐ Ackman sold 95% of his Alphabet (GOOGL) holdings to fund these new investments, though he remains long-term bullish on Alphabet.
вАҐ He favors Microsoft due to its strong cloud (Azure) and software (Microsoft 365) businesses, and its OpenAI stake.
вАҐ Amazon's e-commerce and AWS cloud segments are seen as dominant franchises with long-term growth potential.
вАҐ The fund manager believes software companies tied to customer workflows are AI beneficiaries, not losers.
news.Keywords: Portfolio reallocation, hedge fund activity, AI beneficiaries, cloud computing, enterprise software

news.Title: Nvidia's Latest AI Partner Was a Bargain Hiding in Plain Sight. Is It Still a Buy?
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) investment has supercharged the share prices of tech giants like Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) and Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG) . Over the last three years, since the AI revolution began, their stock prices have surged 117% and 216%, respectively. Meanwhile, chipmaker Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has seen its shares soar more than 597% as it dominated the market for high-end AI processors. But AI has transformed more than just tech giants. It's even electrified the ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ AI investment has significantly boosted the stock prices of tech giants like Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOGL), and chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA).
вАҐ Corning (GLW), a glass manufacturer, has seen its stock surge 259% in the past year due to its new Nvidia partnership and demand for its fiber-optic cables.
вАҐ Corning's fiber-optic business became a high-demand product for AI data centers requiring high bandwidth and flexible cabling.
вАҐ The company's optical communications segment saw net income rise 28% year-over-year in 2024 and 71% year-over-year in 2025.
вАҐ The article also references a report on a company providing critical technology for Nvidia and Intel.
news.Keywords: AI impact, fiber optics, data center demand, semiconductor ecosystem, stock performance

news.Title: 2 Monster Stocks to Hold for the Next 5 Years
news.Content: If you're looking for monster stocks to own for the next five years, you don't have to scour far and wide to find great investments. In fact, some of the best may be right under your nose. These include the stocks of companies whose products or services you use all the time. Let's look at two growth stocks you can buy today and hold at least for the next five years. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Amazon (AMZN) is highlighted as a monster stock for the next five years due to its e-commerce and cloud computing operations.
вАҐ The company utilizes over 1 million robots coordinated by its DeepFleet AI model in its fulfillment centers and is expanding into last-mile delivery robots and humanoid robots.
вАҐ Amazon Web Services (AWS) is its most profitable segment, with revenue up 28% in Q1, driven by demand for computing power and AI services.
вАҐ Amazon is investing $200 billion this year to meet demand, securing large commitments from anchor customers like Anthropic and OpenAI.
вАҐ The article also mentions Apple (AAPL) as another potential long-term holding due to its ecosystem and growing services revenue.
news.Keywords: E-commerce, cloud computing, AI infrastructure, robotics, long-term growth

news.Title: Boom or Bubble? This Is Where Micron Technology Stock Could Be in 5 Years
news.Content: The memory industry has been booming for the past three years, propelled by the proliferation of artificial intelligence (AI), which has created demand for larger, faster memory across multiple applications. Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) has been a major beneficiary of the memory boom. Micron stock has shot up by 935% over the past three years, driven by a big jump in its revenue and earnings. Investors, however, may now be wondering if the memory specialist has now entered bubble territory, especially considering that it has been historically susceptible ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Micron Technology (MU) has benefited significantly from the AI-driven memory boom, with its stock up 935% over three years.
вАҐ The demand for larger, faster memory for AI workloads in data centers, particularly High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM), is driving the current cycle.
вАҐ HBM revenue is projected to reach $55 billion in 2026 and $130 billion by 2030, with data centers consuming 70% of memory supply.
вАҐ Memory manufacturers are prioritizing HBM over DRAM, leading to potential shortages until 2030 due to long fabrication plant lead times.
вАҐ Micron returned to revenue growth and profitability in 2024, with a favorable pricing environment expected to sustain its growth.
news.Keywords: Memory market, AI demand, HBM, supply constraints, semiconductor cycle

news.Title: AMD Expands Taiwan Capacity as AI Tightens CPU Supply
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Advanced Micro Devices ( AMD , Financials ) is moving to expand production capacity in ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expanding its production capacity in Taiwan to meet higher-than-expected CPU demand, indicating AI growth beyond GPUs.
вАҐ CEO Lisa Su stated that the global CPU market is tight, with AMD working with partners to increase supply throughout 2024 and significantly more capacity planned for 2027.
вАҐ Demand is driven by AI inferencing and agentic AI, increasing the need for CPUs in data centers and enterprise systems.
вАҐ AMD plans to invest over $10 billion in Taiwan's AI sector, focusing on advanced packaging, substrates, and rack-scale AI systems.
вАҐ The company is also ramping up production of its Venice CPUs using TSMC's 2-nanometer process technology.
news.Keywords: CPU supply, AI inferencing, data center demand, Taiwan manufacturing, advanced packaging

news.Title: TSMC Trails 140% AI Rallies As Investors Broaden Chip Bets
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. ( NYSE:TSM ) is still one of Asia's most important AI beneficiaries , but investors are no longer treating it ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Investors are broadening their chip investments beyond TSMC (Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.) as AI demand spreads across various hardware segments.
вАҐ TSMC shares are up 46% year-to-date, but MediaTek and Samsung Electronics have seen rallies exceeding 140%.
вАҐ MediaTek is gaining traction for its work on application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs) with Alphabet.
вАҐ Samsung is benefiting from its position as the world's largest memory maker and has joined the $1 trillion market cap club.
вАҐ Fund managers are diversifying due to single-stock limits and TSMC's significant weighting in the Taiwan index, looking at other Asian tech names.
news.Keywords: Chip sector diversification, AI hardware, ASIC development, memory market, investment strategy

news.Title: SpaceX IPO: Why It Could Burst the AI Bubble
news.Content: Talk of an AI bubble has faded since late last year, when signs of weakness in some AI stocks and fears around circular deals triggered a sell-off in names like Nvidia . Since then, a shortage in memory chips and strong demand growth have sent AI stocks surging again, with major indexes hitting all-time highs following the dip over the Iran war. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ SpaceX's IPO filing reveals a potential AI bubble test, with a $2 trillion valuation target.
вАҐ SpaceX reported Q1 revenue growth of 15.4% to $4.7 billion and a GAAP operating loss of $1.9 billion.
вАҐ The company's 2025 revenue was $18.7 billion, with an operating loss of $2.6 billion.
вАҐ A $2 trillion valuation for SpaceX implies a price-to-sales ratio over 100, significantly higher than most S&P 500 stocks.
вАҐ The IPO could either fuel an AI stock frenzy or trigger a sell-off if the valuation proves unsustainable.
news.Keywords: AI bubble, IPO valuation, SpaceX financials, semiconductor cycle, market sentiment

news.Title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock That Has Run Circles Around Nvidia in the Past Year Still Has 39% Upside, According to a Wall Street Analyst
news.Content: Nvidia has been one of the biggest winners of the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution. However, several other companies, including some of Nvidia's competitors, are also capitalizing on AI. One of them is Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) . Over the past year, AMD has significantly outperformed Nvidia on equity markets. The former has gained 296% as of writing, compared to the latter's 63%. What's more, there are reasons to ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has significantly outperformed Nvidia in the past year, gaining 296% versus Nvidia's 63%.
вАҐ A Wall Street analyst has set a $625 price target for AMD, suggesting a 39% upside.
вАҐ AMD's server CPU market share increased to 33.2% in Q1, up 5% year-over-year, driven by demand for its EPYC processor in agentic AI.
вАҐ In Q1, AMD's revenue increased by 38% year-over-year to $10.3 billion, with data center revenue up 57% to $5.8 billion.
вАҐ AMD projects the server CPU market to grow over 35% annually through 2030, reaching over $120 billion.
news.Keywords: AI, semiconductor, market share, revenue growth, price target

news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Tesla faces renewed speculation of a potential merger with SpaceX following SpaceX's IPO filing.
вАҐ Wedbush analyst Dan Ives suggests a merger could occur by next year, consolidating Elon Musk's ventures.
вАҐ Traders show reduced confidence in a Tesla-SpaceX merger, with odds for a deal before May 2027 dropping significantly.
вАҐ A merger could offer Tesla a broader technology narrative amidst increasing competition in China.
вАҐ Complex governance issues and the need for board approvals from both companies would need to be addressed for any combination.
news.Keywords: merger speculation, corporate structure, AI infrastructure, competitive landscape, governance

news.Title: Wall Street's Favorite New AI Stock vs. the One They're Ignoring: The Numbers Aren't Even Close
news.Content: One of Wall Street's favorite artificial intelligence (AI) stocks right now is, no surprise, the semiconductor company Cerebras (NASDAQ: CBRS) . It's been a publicly traded company for only about a week, but enthusiasm is high. Investors rushing in to buy the stock have pushed it up by about 60% from its $185 IPO price as of this writing. But not all AI stocks are feeling the love right now, especially not Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) . Despite solid growth in ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Microsoft reported fiscal Q3 2026 sales of nearly $83 billion, an 18% year-over-year increase, with non-GAAP earnings up 21% to $4.27 per share.
вАҐ In contrast, Cerebras reported 2025 sales of only $500 million and a non-GAAP loss of almost $76 million.
вАҐ Microsoft's stock has declined over 8% in the past 12 months, while Cerebras' stock has surged about 60% from its IPO price.
вАҐ The article contrasts the financial performance of Microsoft and Cerebras, suggesting investors may be overlooking Microsoft's strong fundamentals in favor of the newer AI stock.

news.Title: OpenAI Is Going Public But Is It for the Wrong Reasons?
news.Content: The AI sector is about to get even bigger. According to several media reports, OpenAI , the ChatGPT creator is aiming to file confidentially for an IPO in the coming weeks with the goal of going public as soon as September. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ¬ї The news comes as another blockbuster IPO, SpaceX, is headed for the public ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ OpenAI is reportedly planning a confidential IPO in the coming weeks, aiming to go public as soon as September.
вАҐ Anthropic, a competitor, is in talks to raise funding at a valuation between $900 billion and $950 billion, potentially surpassing OpenAI's last valuation of $852 billion.
вАҐ Anthropic's revenue is expected to more than double from $4.8 billion in Q1 to $10.9 billion in Q2, while OpenAI reached annualized revenue of $25 billion in March, up 17% year-to-date.
вАҐ OpenAI is facing a significant cash crunch, with substantial future spending commitments and projected cash flow losses until 2030, suggesting the IPO may be driven by necessity rather than strength.
news.Keywords: IPO, AI, Valuation, Cash Burn, Competition

news.Title: Investors Look Beyond TSMC as AI Boom Spreads to New Winners
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- After several years flying high as AsiaвАЩs best Nvidia Corp. proxy, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. is increasingly vying with other artificial intelligence stocks for investor attention. Most Read from Bloomberg Bungie Plans Layoffs After Ending вАШDestiny 2вАЩ Development Spot the Difference: Putin Gets Trump Treatment From Xi in China Iran Says the USвАЩs Latest Proposal Has вАШNarrowed the GapsвАЩ Iran in Talks With Oman Over Permanent Hormuz Toll System ModiвАЩs Toffee Gift to Meloni Ignites Rally in Wrong Indian Stock Stock traders are chasing a wider array of beneficiaries ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is facing increased competition for investor attention from other AI beneficiaries.
вАҐ While TSMC shares are up 46% this year, MediaTek and Samsung Electronics have seen gains exceeding 140%.
вАҐ TSMC's underperformance is attributed to a diversification trend away from the company as AI adoption broadens.
вАҐ The rise of agentic AI is driving demand for a wider range of chips, including CPUs, benefiting companies like MediaTek and Samsung.
вАҐ Investment caps and the desire for diversification are pushing funds to explore alternatives beyond TSMC, impacting its relative performance.
news.Keywords: AI beneficiaries, diversification, semiconductor market, chip designers, market performance

news.Title: Analysis-ECB, banks rift hampers Europe's efforts to loosen reliance on US payments giants
news.Content: By Jes√Їs Aguado and Valentina Za MADRID/MILAN, May 22 (Reuters) - Europe's push to curb its dependence on U.S. payments giants Visa and Mastercard has driven a wedge between the European Central Bank and financial firms keen to shield revenues, hobbling efforts to build a home-grown system, several people involved said. More from Yahoo Scout How are European banks resisting the digital euro? What is Europe's digital euro initiative and timeline? What revenue impact would digital euro have? Why is Europe seeking payment system sovereignty? A ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Europe's efforts to reduce reliance on U.S. payment giants like Visa and Mastercard are being hampered by disagreements between the European Central Bank (ECB) and financial firms.
вАҐ The ECB plans to introduce a digital euro by 2029, but European banks are concerned about potential customer fund transfers to an ECB-guaranteed wallet and are exploring alternative solutions.
вАҐ A proposed cap on merchant fees for digital euro transactions could cost the private payments system an estimated 8 billion to 9 billion euros in lost annual revenues.
вАҐ The development of a home-grown European payment system faces challenges due to misaligned incentives and timelines between public and private actors, potentially impacting revenue streams for financial institutions.
news.Keywords: Payment Systems, Digital Euro, Regulatory Hurdles, Merchant Fees, Financial Sovereignty

news.Title: No, Tesla Doesn't Need E.U. Approval to Sell Full Self-Driving (Supervised) in Europe
news.Content: Obtaining approval to sell its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software in Europe is one of the key catalysts for Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) stock in 2026. Not only are FSD (Supervised) and other software services becoming an important revenue generator for the company, but the demonstration of FSD (Supervised) is a key precursor of the potential approval of robotaxis using unsupervised FSD in the future. And the good news is that Tesla doesn't need European Union-wide approval to ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Tesla does not require a single EU-wide approval to sell its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software in Europe; it can pursue country-by-country approvals.
вАҐ The Netherlands has already approved Tesla's FSD (Supervised) via its Vehicle Authority (RDW), setting a precedent for other countries.
вАҐ An EU-wide vote on FSD (Supervised) approval is anticipated in July, but individual countries can grant provisional approvals based on existing data, potentially speeding up market entry.
вАҐ The approval of FSD (Supervised) is considered a key catalyst for Tesla's stock, paving the way for potential robotaxi approvals in the future.
news.Keywords: Autonomous Driving, Regulatory Approval, Market Entry, Software Services, Catalysts

news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ еНЧдЇЮзІСзЄљзґУзРЖжЭОеЯєиЛ±и°®з§ЇпЉМзЫЃеЙНDRAMзФҐеУБжѓЫеИ©зОЗжЬЙж©ЯжЬГжѓФHBMйЂШпЉМдЄ¶йАПйЬ≤еЕђеПЄеРИдљЬзЪДAIеЃҐжИґдЄНеП™дЄАеЃґгАВ
вАҐ еЃҐжИґе∞НйХЈзіДйЬАж±ВжЛЙйХЈпЉМйГ®еИЖз≠ЦзХ•е§•дЉіеЈ≤з∞љи®ВдЄЙеєійХЈжЬЯеРИзіДпЉМеНЧдЇЮзІСе∞ЗеЕ®еКЫжїњиґ≥еЃҐжИґйЬАж±ВгАВ
вАҐ еПЧжГ†жЦЉжЉ≤еГєпЉМеНЧдЇЮзІС4жЬИзЗЯжФґйБФ255еДДеЕГпЉМжЬИеҐЮиґЕйБО4жИРпЉМй°ѓз§ЇеЇЂе≠Шж∞ідљНдљОдЄФеЗЇи≤®йЗПеПЧйЩРжЦЉзФҐиГљгАВ
вАҐ еЕђеПЄи®ИеКГ5AжЦ∞еї†жЦЉжШОеєізђђдЄАе≠£йЦЛеІЛи£Эж©ЯпЉМдЄЛеНКеєійЗПзФҐпЉМзЫЃж®ЩдЄЙеєіеЊМдљНеЕГзФҐеЗЇжО•ињСзњїеАНгАВ
вАҐ еЃМжИРзІБеЛЯж°ИеЛЯи≥ЗзіД787еДДеЕГпЉМе∞ЗжКХеЕ•еЕИйА≤и®ШжЖґйЂФи£љйА†еї†еЛЩиИЗи®≠еВЩеїЇзљЃпЉМжКЉж≥®AIжЩВдї£зЪДжЦ∞и®ШжЖґйЂФйЬАж±ВгАВ

news.Title: AMD ramping up Taiwan capacity as global CPU market tightens
news.Content: By Wen-Yee Lee and Ben Blanchard TAIPEI, May 22 (Reuters) - Advanced Micro Devices is working with Taiwan partners to ramp up production capacity as stronger-than-expected demand squeezes the global CPU market, CEO Lisa Su said on Friday. Speaking in вАМTaipei after a visit to China, Su said she had met AMD's largest customers in China and globally ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ AMD is increasing its production capacity in Taiwan with local partners due to higher-than-expected global CPU demand.
вАҐ CEO Lisa Su stated the CPU market is currently 'tight' and expects supply to increase quarterly throughout 2024, with significant expansion planned for 2027 and beyond.
вАҐ Growth drivers include AI inferencing and agentic AI, broadening demand for CPUs beyond traditional GPU applications.
вАҐ AMD plans to invest over $10 billion in Taiwan's AI sector, focusing on advanced packaging, substrates, and manufacturing for rack-scale systems, and is ramping production of Venice CPUs using TSMC's 2-nanometer process.
news.Keywords: CPU Market, Supply Chain, AI Demand, Manufacturing Capacity, Strategic Investment

news.Title: Nvidia Just Raised Its Dividend By 2,400% -- and That Wasn't Even the Biggest News From Its Earnings Report
news.Content: AI chipmaker Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) managed to largely hold onto a sharp gain over the last 30 days when it reported earnings. Though the stock fell about 1.8% during the first trading day following its report, the stock is up 9.8% over the last 30 days. The quarterly numbers and the outlook for the period ahead were both exceptional. But tucked into the same release was a capital return ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Nvidia reported fiscal Q1 2026 revenue of $81.6 billion, an 85% year-over-year increase and a 20% sequential climb, marking accelerated growth for the third consecutive quarter.
вАҐ The data center segment generated a record $75.2 billion in revenue, up 92% year-over-year, with networking revenue soaring 199% to $14.8 billion.
вАҐ Non-GAAP earnings per share rose 140% to $1.87, and adjusted gross margin expanded to 75%.
вАҐ Nvidia announced a 2,400% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.25 per share and authorized an additional $80 billion in share repurchases, alongside generating $48.6 billion in free cash flow.
news.Keywords: Earnings Report, Revenue Growth, Data Center, Profitability, Capital Return

news.Title: Rigetti Computing (RGTI) Soars 30% on $100M US Govt Backing
news.Content: Rigetti Computing Inc. (NASDAQ:RGTI) is one of the 10 Stocks With Shocking Overnight Gains. Rigetti Computing climbed by 30.57 percent on Thursday to close at $22.04 apiece, as investors snapped up shares after the ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Rigetti Computing (RGTI) shares surged 30.57% following the announcement of US government backing for quantum computer development.
вАҐ The Department of Commerce will provide $2.013 billion in federal incentives under the CHIPS and Science Act for nine companies, including seven quantum computing firms and two foundries.
вАҐ Rigetti Computing will receive up to $100 million to develop next-generation superconducting quantum computing technologies.
вАҐ IBM and GlobalFoundries are also receiving significant funding, with IBM getting $1 billion and GlobalFoundries $375 million.
news.Keywords: Quantum Computing, Government Funding, CHIPS Act, Semiconductor, Technology Development
