news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Arm Holdings (ARM) reached an all-time high, driven by an optimistic outlook for fivefold profit growth by 2030.

news.Title: Apple asks US Supreme Court to review contempt order in Epic Games lawsuit
news.Content: By Mike Scarcella WASHINGTON, May 21 (Reuters) - Apple asked the U.S. Supreme Court on Thursday to review a lower court ruling that held the iPhone maker in вАМcivil contempt over fees it was charging on some outside purchases made by ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Main Street can now bet on OpenAl, SpaceX through Polymarket
news.Content: The biggest financial story of the last decade is not what is happening on Wall Street . It is what is happening just outside of it. The most valuable companies of this generation, the ones running cloud infrastructure, satellite internet, rocket launches and a sizable chunk of artificial intelligence research, have spent years deliberately staying private. They have raised tens of billions of dollars without ever filing an S-1. For most working Americans, that has been a closed loop. You can buy Tesla ( TSLA ) stock through your brokerage account today. You cannot buy SpaceX stock, no matter how ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Prediction market platform Polymarket has launched event contracts tied to the performance of private companies like OpenAI and SpaceX.
вАҐ This initiative, in partnership with Nasdaq Private Market, allows individuals to bet on milestones and valuations of these pre-IPO companies.
вАҐ The platform aims to provide access to the performance of high-value private companies, which have historically been inaccessible to most investors.
вАҐ Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), the parent company of the NYSE, has invested significantly in Polymarket, indicating strategic interest in this parallel market for sentiment.
news.Keywords: private markets, prediction markets, venture capital, alternative investments, market sentiment

news.Title: The Path to 'Peak' Nvidia: Why the AI Giant's Future Will Inevitably Include Stiff Competition
news.Content: Wall Street has already grown thoroughly accustomed to the nearly non-stop expansion of Nvidia's (NVDA) market capitalization. The company has firmly established itself at the absolute summit of tech Olympus. Right now, current financial conditions for the business look ideal. Demand for artificial intelligence (AI) computing power exceeds supply by massive multiples, margins are breaking historical records, and financial flows seem completely inexhaustible. But my fundamental analysis requires a different approach. Investors shouldn't just look at the current point of peak triumph, but beyond the horizon. That's where the trends for the next three to five ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Nvidia (NVDA) currently enjoys peak market conditions with high demand for AI computing power, record margins, and strong financial flows.
вАҐ However, the long-term outlook suggests an environment of fierce competition, indicating the end of Nvidia's absolute dominance.
вАҐ Nvidia's competitive advantage, built on its CUDA software ecosystem, is being challenged by the rise of specialized ASICs developed by major clients like Microsoft (MSFT) and Meta Platforms (META) for cost optimization.
вАҐ Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is also posing a threat with its open-source ROCm software platform, aiming to capture market share in the GPU space.
news.Keywords: AI market, competitive landscape, software ecosystem, proprietary silicon, market share erosion

news.Title: Seagate stock price falls after CEO's rattling warning
news.Content: A throwaway line from a CEO rarely moves an entire sector in a single afternoon. Dave Mosley managed it on Monday. Speaking at the JPMorgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference, as reported by Benzinga , the Seagate Technology ( STX ) chief was asked a simple question: would the company build new factories to chase soaring AI storage demand? His answer rattled the room. New plants " would just take too long ," he said, and could leave Seagate with more capacity than it needs. That single comment dragged down the whole memory group, pulling Micron ( MU ), ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Seagate Technology (STX) stock fell significantly after its CEO warned about long lead times for recording head wafers (over nine months) and finished drives (an additional quarter).
вАҐ The CEO's comments suggested that Seagate cannot build new factories quickly enough to meet soaring AI storage demand, potentially leaving the company with excess capacity if demand shifts.
вАҐ The warning negatively impacted other memory stocks, including Micron (MU), Western Digital (WDC), and SanDisk (SNDK), which also experienced declines.
вАҐ Investors are debating whether Seagate's growth is supply-constrained or demand-constrained, with the former implying a ceiling on upside potential due to production limitations.
news.Keywords: storage demand, supply chain constraints, wafer lead times, AI infrastructure, memory market

news.Title: Has Nvidia Become Too Big? 3 Alternative AI Stocks
news.Content: Once again, Nvidia ( NVDA ) delivered a record quarterly earnings report, with profits growing 140% year over year and revenue rising 85%, both ahead of analyst expectations. One particularly interesting development was the introduction of a new reporting segment: Edge. In classic Jensen Huang fashion, Nvidia appears to already be positioning for the next evolution of AI, recognizing that the long-term opportunity extends beyond massive data centers and into AI running directly on personal devices and at the network edge. Nvidia stock has been on an extraordinary run, climbing roughly 1,500% since bottoming in late 2022. Yet despite that ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Nvidia (NVDA) reported record quarterly earnings with profits up 140% YoY and revenue up 85% YoY, exceeding analyst expectations.

news.Title: Stocks Finish Sharply Higher on Iran Hopes
news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) on Wednesday closed up +1.08%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) closed up +1.31%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) closed up +1.66%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) rose +0.96%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) rose +1.57%. Stock indexes finished sharply higher on Wednesday, recovering most of this weekвАЩs losses, amid a plunge in crude oil prices and lower bond yields. Crude oil prices sank by more than 5% on hopes for a deal to end the Iran war, knocking inflation expectations and bond yields lower. The 10-year T-note yield fell ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Major US stock indexes (S&P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq 100) closed sharply higher, recovering weekly losses.
вАҐ The rally was driven by a significant drop in crude oil prices (over 5%) due to hopes for a resolution in the Iran conflict, which lowered inflation expectations and bond yields.
вАҐ Semiconductor stocks also rallied, with Nvidia showing a gain ahead of its earnings report, which is expected to provide insights into the AI economy.
вАҐ FOMC meeting minutes indicated a hawkish stance, with some policymakers considering interest rate increases if inflation persists above 2%.
вАҐ Q1 earnings for S&P 500 companies are projected to climb +12% year-over-year, with 83% of reporting companies beating estimates.
news.Keywords: market rally, crude oil prices, bond yields, semiconductor stocks, inflation expectations

news.Title: Cisco stock flashes rare technical signal

The stock had spent years in the slow lane - a legacy networking name that the market had largely written off while flashier AI plays grabbed the headlines. Fast forward to now, and that bet has nearly doubled. As of Friday's close, according to TheStreet Pro , CSCO was up 98.6% from an entry point recommended by analyst Ed Ponsi in 2024. news.Title: How Big Could the AI Data Center Opportunity Be for Navitas?
news.Content: The artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure is set to become a major long-term growth catalyst for Navitas Semiconductor NVTS as hyperscalers are increasing power requirements inside next-generation data centers. This shift is creating a large and fast-growing opportunity for the companyвАЩs gallium nitride (GaN) and silicon carbide (SiC) power semiconductor portfolio. The company estimates the AI ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) is positioned to benefit from the AI infrastructure boom, with the AI data center market estimated to be a $1.4-$2.5 billion SAM opportunity by 2030.
вАҐ The company projects GaN and SiC adoption in AI data centers to grow at a 66-87% CAGR between 2025 and 2030, driven by efficiency and power density demands.
вАҐ Navitas estimates potential GaN and SiC semiconductor content of $25,000-$35,000 per megawatt in future AI infrastructure deployments.
вАҐ AI infrastructure revenues for Navitas grew 50% sequentially in Q1 2026, exceeding expectations, and the company believes its dual GaN/SiC offering provides a strategic advantage.
news.Keywords: AI data centers, power semiconductors, GaN, SiC, market opportunity

news.Title: ON Semiconductor Stock Soars 60% in 3 Months: Still a Smart Bet?
news.Content: ON Semiconductor Corporation ON has been one of the semiconductor sectorвАЩs strongest performers lately, with the stock surging 60.1% over the past three months, compared with the industryвАЩs rise of 29.2%. The rally has been fueled by a combination of improving cyclical demand, accelerating artificial intelligence data-center opportunities and rising confidence that the company has moved beyond the worst phase of the semiconductor downturn. Investors were especially encouraged by ON SemiconductorвАЩs first-quarter 2026 results, where both revenues and earnings exceeded expectations while management projected stronger growth ahead. A major driver behind the stockвАЩs sharp ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ ON Semiconductor (ON) stock has surged 60.1% in three months, outperforming the semiconductor sector, driven by improving cyclical demand and AI data center opportunities.
вАҐ First-quarter 2026 results exceeded expectations, with AI data center revenues up over 30% sequentially and projected to double year-over-year in 2026.
вАҐ The company is a key beneficiary of AI infrastructure growth, supplying power-management chips for AI servers and hyperscale data centers.
вАҐ Automotive semiconductor revenues reached $797 million in Q1 2026, returning to growth, with significant traction in EVs, especially in China, and adoption of its Treo platform for next-gen vehicle architectures.
вАҐ Gross margin expanded to 38.5% in Q1 2026, with expectations for further improvement due to rising utilization and higher-margin product mix.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, automotive semiconductors, power management, cyclical recovery, margin expansion

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Stocks Mixed as Crude Oil Prices and Bond Yields Fall
news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) today is up +0.07%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) is up +0.29%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) is down -0.25%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) are up +0.10%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) are down -0.17%. Stock index futures recovered from overnight losses and are trading mixed as crude oil prices fell more than -1% on hopes for a breakthrough in the standoff between the US and Iran over the Strait of Hormuz. Crude prices retreated today after Tasnim, Iran's semi-official news agency, reported that the US has proposed ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Stock markets are mixed as crude oil prices and bond yields fluctuate due to geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran, and news regarding potential oil sanctions waivers.
вАҐ Geopolitical concerns, including troop deployments to Saudi Arabia and drone incidents, are impacting market sentiment.
вАҐ Weaker-than-expected economic data from China, particularly in industrial production and retail sales, is adding bearish pressure on global growth prospects.
вАҐ Earnings season is largely concluded, with 83% of S&P 500 companies beating Q1 estimates, though earnings growth excluding technology is weaker.
news.Keywords: macroeconomic factors, geopolitical risk, oil prices, bond yields, earnings season

news.Content: Silicon Motion Technology Corporation (NASDAQ: SIMO ) was among Jim CramerвАЩs Mad Money stock calls as he urged investors to exercise caution when it comes to red-hot AI stocks . When a caller asked about the stock, Cramer stated: That is called SIMOвА¶ And we like SIMO. I know itвАЩs ...
news.AI_Summary:
вАҐ Jim Cramer recommends buying Silicon Motion (SIMO) but suggests waiting for a pullback due to its recent significant price appreciation.
вАҐ Silicon Motion develops and markets NAND flash controllers and specialized storage solutions for computing, enterprise data centers, and mobile devices.
вАҐ The company has transitioned towards higher-margin, leading-edge applications driven by hyperscaler demands, moving away from lower-margin consumer electronics.
вАҐ Heartland Advisors believes SIMO remains undervalued, with a price target of $185 compared to its current trading price of $117, factoring in an anticipated $160 million cash settlement from an arbitration case.
